1	from io import IOBase
2	
3	from django.conf import settings
4	from django.core import signals
5	from django.core.handlers import base
6	from django.http import HttpRequest, QueryDict, parse_cookie
7	from django.urls import set_script_prefix
8	from django.utils.encoding import repercent_broken_unicode
9	from django.utils.functional import cached_property
10	from django.utils.regex_helper import _lazy_re_compile
11	
12	_slashes_re = _lazy_re_compile(rb"/+")
13	
14	
15	class LimitedStream(IOBase):
16	    """
17	    Wrap another stream to disallow reading it past a number of bytes.
18	
19	    Based on the implementation from werkzeug.wsgi.LimitedStream. See:
20	    https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/blob/dbf78f67/src/werkzeug/wsgi.py#L828
21	    """
22	
23	    def __init__(self, stream, limit):
24	        self._read = stream.read
25	        self._readline = stream.readline
26	        self._pos = 0
27	        self.limit = limit
28	
29	    def read(self, size=-1, /):
30	        _pos = self._pos
31	        limit = self.limit
32	        if _pos >= limit:
33	            return b""
34	        if size == -1 or size is None:
35	            size = limit - _pos
36	        else:
37	            size = min(size, limit - _pos)
38	        data = self._read(size)
39	        self._pos += len(data)
40	        return data
41	
42	    def readline(self, size=-1, /):
43	        _pos = self._pos
44	        limit = self.limit
45	        if _pos >= limit:
46	            return b""
47	        if size == -1 or size is None:
48	            size = limit - _pos
49	        else:
50	            size = min(size, limit - _pos)
51	        line = self._readline(size)
52	        self._pos += len(line)
53	        return line
54	
55	
56	class WSGIRequest(HttpRequest):
57	    def __init__(self, environ):
58	        script_name = get_script_name(environ)
59	        # If PATH_INFO is empty (e.g. accessing the SCRIPT_NAME URL without a
60	        # trailing slash), operate as if '/' was requested.
61	        path_info = get_path_info(environ) or "/"
62	        self.environ = environ
63	        self.path_info = path_info
64	        # be careful to only replace the first slash in the path because of
65	        # http://test/something and http://test//something being different as
66	        # stated in RFC 3986.
67	        self.path = "%s/%s" % (script_name.rstrip("/"), path_info.replace("/", "", 1))
68	        self.META = environ
69	        self.META["PATH_INFO"] = path_info
70	        self.META["SCRIPT_NAME"] = script_name
71	        self.method = environ["REQUEST_METHOD"].upper()
72	        # Set content_type, content_params, and encoding.
73	        self._set_content_type_params(environ)
74	        try:
75	            content_length = int(environ.get("CONTENT_LENGTH"))
76	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
77	            content_length = 0
78	        self._stream = LimitedStream(self.environ["wsgi.input"], content_length)
79	        self._read_started = False
80	        self.resolver_match = None
81	
82	    def _get_scheme(self):
83	        return self.environ.get("wsgi.url_scheme")
84	
85	    @cached_property
86	    def GET(self):
87	        # The WSGI spec says 'QUERY_STRING' may be absent.
88	        raw_query_string = get_bytes_from_wsgi(self.environ, "QUERY_STRING", "")
89	        return QueryDict(raw_query_string, encoding=self._encoding)
90	
91	    def _get_post(self):
92	        if not hasattr(self, "_post"):
93	            self._load_post_and_files()
94	        return self._post
95	
96	    def _set_post(self, post):
97	        self._post = post
98	
99	    @cached_property
100	    def COOKIES(self):
101	        raw_cookie = get_str_from_wsgi(self.environ, "HTTP_COOKIE", "")
102	        return parse_cookie(raw_cookie)
103	
104	    @property
105	    def FILES(self):
106	        if not hasattr(self, "_files"):
107	            self._load_post_and_files()
108	        return self._files
109	
110	    POST = property(_get_post, _set_post)
111	
112	
113	class WSGIHandler(base.BaseHandler):
114	    request_class = WSGIRequest
115	
116	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
117	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
118	        self.load_middleware()
119	
120	    def __call__(self, environ, start_response):
121	        set_script_prefix(get_script_name(environ))
122	        signals.request_started.send(sender=self.__class__, environ=environ)
123	        request = self.request_class(environ)
124	        response = self.get_response(request)
125	
126	        response._handler_class = self.__class__
127	
128	        status = "%d %s" % (response.status_code, response.reason_phrase)
129	        response_headers = [
130	            *response.items(),
131	            *(("Set-Cookie", c.OutputString()) for c in response.cookies.values()),
132	        ]
133	        start_response(status, response_headers)
134	        if getattr(response, "file_to_stream", None) is not None and environ.get(
135	            "wsgi.file_wrapper"
136	        ):
137	            # If `wsgi.file_wrapper` is used the WSGI server does not call
138	            # .close on the response, but on the file wrapper. Patch it to use
139	            # response.close instead which takes care of closing all files.
140	            response.file_to_stream.close = response.close
141	            response = environ["wsgi.file_wrapper"](
142	                response.file_to_stream, response.block_size
143	            )
144	        return response
145	
146	
147	def get_path_info(environ):
148	    """Return the HTTP request's PATH_INFO as a string."""
149	    path_info = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "PATH_INFO", "/")
150	
151	    return repercent_broken_unicode(path_info).decode()
152	
153	
154	def get_script_name(environ):
155	    """
156	    Return the equivalent of the HTTP request's SCRIPT_NAME environment
157	    variable. If Apache mod_rewrite is used, return what would have been
158	    the script name prior to any rewriting (so it's the script name as seen
159	    from the client's perspective), unless the FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME setting is
160	    set (to anything).
161	    """
162	    if settings.FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME is not None:
163	        return settings.FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME
164	
165	    # If Apache's mod_rewrite had a whack at the URL, Apache set either
166	    # SCRIPT_URL or REDIRECT_URL to the full resource URL before applying any
167	    # rewrites. Unfortunately not every web server (lighttpd!) passes this
168	    # information through all the time, so FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME, above, is still
169	    # needed.
170	    script_url = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "SCRIPT_URL", "") or get_bytes_from_wsgi(
171	        environ, "REDIRECT_URL", ""
172	    )
173	
174	    if script_url:
175	        if b"//" in script_url:
176	            # mod_wsgi squashes multiple successive slashes in PATH_INFO,
177	            # do the same with script_url before manipulating paths (#17133).
178	            script_url = _slashes_re.sub(b"/", script_url)
179	        path_info = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "PATH_INFO", "")
180	        script_name = script_url.removesuffix(path_info)
181	    else:
182	        script_name = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "SCRIPT_NAME", "")
183	
184	    return script_name.decode()
185	
186	
187	def get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, key, default):
188	    """
189	    Get a value from the WSGI environ dictionary as bytes.
190	
191	    key and default should be strings.
192	    """
193	    value = environ.get(key, default)
194	    # Non-ASCII values in the WSGI environ are arbitrarily decoded with
195	    # ISO-8859-1. This is wrong for Django websites where UTF-8 is the default.
196	    # Re-encode to recover the original bytestring.
197	    return value.encode("iso-8859-1")
198	
199	
200	def get_str_from_wsgi(environ, key, default):
201	    """
202	    Get a value from the WSGI environ dictionary as str.
203	
204	    key and default should be str objects.
205	    """
206	    value = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, key, default)
207	    return value.decode(errors="replace")

1	import asyncio
2	import logging
3	import types
4	from inspect import iscoroutinefunction
5	
6	from asgiref.sync import async_to_sync, sync_to_async
7	
8	from django.conf import settings
9	from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, MiddlewareNotUsed
10	from django.core.signals import request_finished
11	from django.db import connections, transaction
12	from django.urls import get_resolver, set_urlconf
13	from django.utils.log import log_response
14	from django.utils.module_loading import import_string
15	
16	from .exception import convert_exception_to_response
17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger("django.request")
19	
20	
21	class BaseHandler:
22	    _view_middleware = None
23	    _template_response_middleware = None
24	    _exception_middleware = None
25	    _middleware_chain = None
26	
27	    def load_middleware(self, is_async=False):
28	        """
29	        Populate middleware lists from settings.MIDDLEWARE.
30	
31	        Must be called after the environment is fixed (see __call__ in
32	        subclasses).
33	        """
34	        self._view_middleware = []
35	        self._template_response_middleware = []
36	        self._exception_middleware = []
37	
38	        get_response = self._get_response_async if is_async else self._get_response
39	        handler = convert_exception_to_response(get_response)
40	        handler_is_async = is_async
41	        for middleware_path in reversed(settings.MIDDLEWARE):
42	            middleware = import_string(middleware_path)
43	            middleware_can_sync = getattr(middleware, "sync_capable", True)
44	            middleware_can_async = getattr(middleware, "async_capable", False)
45	            if not middleware_can_sync and not middleware_can_async:
46	                raise RuntimeError(
47	                    "Middleware %s must have at least one of "
48	                    "sync_capable/async_capable set to True." % middleware_path
49	                )
50	            elif not handler_is_async and middleware_can_sync:
51	                middleware_is_async = False
52	            else:
53	                middleware_is_async = middleware_can_async
54	            try:
55	                # Adapt handler, if needed.
56	                adapted_handler = self.adapt_method_mode(
57	                    middleware_is_async,
58	                    handler,
59	                    handler_is_async,
60	                    debug=settings.DEBUG,
61	                    name="middleware %s" % middleware_path,
62	                )
63	                mw_instance = middleware(adapted_handler)
64	            except MiddlewareNotUsed as exc:
65	                if settings.DEBUG:
66	                    if str(exc):
67	                        logger.debug("MiddlewareNotUsed(%r): %s", middleware_path, exc)
68	                    else:
69	                        logger.debug("MiddlewareNotUsed: %r", middleware_path)
70	                continue
71	            else:
72	                handler = adapted_handler
73	
74	            if mw_instance is None:
75	                raise ImproperlyConfigured(
76	                    "Middleware factory %s returned None." % middleware_path
77	                )
78	
79	            if hasattr(mw_instance, "process_view"):
80	                self._view_middleware.insert(
81	                    0,
82	                    self.adapt_method_mode(is_async, mw_instance.process_view),
83	                )
84	            if hasattr(mw_instance, "process_template_response"):
85	                self._template_response_middleware.append(
86	                    self.adapt_method_mode(
87	                        is_async, mw_instance.process_template_response
88	                    ),
89	                )
90	            if hasattr(mw_instance, "process_exception"):
91	                # The exception-handling stack is still always synchronous for
92	                # now, so adapt that way.
93	                self._exception_middleware.append(
94	                    self.adapt_method_mode(False, mw_instance.process_exception),
95	                )
96	
97	            handler = convert_exception_to_response(mw_instance)
98	            handler_is_async = middleware_is_async
99	
100	        # Adapt the top of the stack, if needed.
101	        handler = self.adapt_method_mode(is_async, handler, handler_is_async)
102	        # We only assign to this when initialization is complete as it is used
103	        # as a flag for initialization being complete.
104	        self._middleware_chain = handler
105	
106	    def adapt_method_mode(
107	        self,
108	        is_async,
109	        method,
110	        method_is_async=None,
111	        debug=False,
112	        name=None,
113	    ):
114	        """
115	        Adapt a method to be in the correct "mode":
116	        - If is_async is False:
117	          - Synchronous methods are left alone
118	          - Asynchronous methods are wrapped with async_to_sync
119	        - If is_async is True:
120	          - Synchronous methods are wrapped with sync_to_async()
121	          - Asynchronous methods are left alone
122	        """
123	        if method_is_async is None:
124	            method_is_async = iscoroutinefunction(method)
125	        if debug and not name:
126	            name = name or "method %s()" % method.__qualname__
127	        if is_async:
128	            if not method_is_async:
129	                if debug:
130	                    logger.debug("Synchronous handler adapted for %s.", name)
131	                return sync_to_async(method, thread_sensitive=True)
132	        elif method_is_async:
133	            if debug:
134	                logger.debug("Asynchronous handler adapted for %s.", name)
135	            return async_to_sync(method)
136	        return method
137	
138	    def get_response(self, request):
139	        """Return an HttpResponse object for the given HttpRequest."""
140	        # Setup default url resolver for this thread
141	        set_urlconf(settings.ROOT_URLCONF)
142	        response = self._middleware_chain(request)
143	        response._resource_closers.append(request.close)
144	        if response.status_code >= 400:
145	            log_response(
146	                "%s: %s",
147	                response.reason_phrase,
148	                request.path,
149	                response=response,
150	                request=request,
151	            )
152	        return response
153	
154	    async def get_response_async(self, request):
155	        """
156	        Asynchronous version of get_response.
157	
158	        Funneling everything, including WSGI, into a single async
159	        get_response() is too slow. Avoid the context switch by using
160	        a separate async response path.
161	        """
162	        # Setup default url resolver for this thread.
163	        set_urlconf(settings.ROOT_URLCONF)
164	        response = await self._middleware_chain(request)
165	        response._resource_closers.append(request.close)
166	        if response.status_code >= 400:
167	            await sync_to_async(log_response, thread_sensitive=False)(
168	                "%s: %s",
169	                response.reason_phrase,
170	                request.path,
171	                response=response,
172	                request=request,
173	            )
174	        return response
175	
176	    def _get_response(self, request):
177	        """
178	        Resolve and call the view, then apply view, exception, and
179	        template_response middleware. This method is everything that happens
180	        inside the request/response middleware.
181	        """
182	        response = None
183	        callback, callback_args, callback_kwargs = self.resolve_request(request)
184	
185	        # Apply view middleware
186	        for middleware_method in self._view_middleware:
187	            response = middleware_method(
188	                request, callback, callback_args, callback_kwargs
189	            )
190	            if response:
191	                break
192	
193	        if response is None:
194	            wrapped_callback = self.make_view_atomic(callback)
195	            # If it is an asynchronous view, run it in a subthread.
196	            if iscoroutinefunction(wrapped_callback):
197	                wrapped_callback = async_to_sync(wrapped_callback)
198	            try:
199	                response = wrapped_callback(request, *callback_args, **callback_kwargs)
200	            except Exception as e:
201	                response = self.process_exception_by_middleware(e, request)
202	                if response is None:
203	                    raise
204	
205	        # Complain if the view returned None (a common error).
206	        self.check_response(response, callback)
207	
208	        # If the response supports deferred rendering, apply template
209	        # response middleware and then render the response
210	        if hasattr(response, "render") and callable(response.render):
211	            for middleware_method in self._template_response_middleware:
212	                response = middleware_method(request, response)
213	                # Complain if the template response middleware returned None
214	                # (a common error).
215	                self.check_response(
216	                    response,
217	                    middleware_method,
218	                    name="%s.process_template_response"
219	                    % (middleware_method.__self__.__class__.__name__,),
220	                )
221	            try:
222	                response = response.render()
223	            except Exception as e:
224	                response = self.process_exception_by_middleware(e, request)
225	                if response is None:
226	                    raise
227	
228	        return response
229	
230	    async def _get_response_async(self, request):
231	        """

315	        resolver_match = resolver.resolve(request.path_info)

320	        """

322	        """
323	        if not (response is None or asyncio.iscoroutine(response)):
324	            return

327	                name = "The view %s.%s" % (callback.__module__, callback.__name__)
328	            else:  # CBV

15	class LimitedStream(IOBase):
16	    """
17	    Wrap another stream to disallow reading it past a number of bytes.
18	
19	    Based on the implementation from werkzeug.wsgi.LimitedStream. See:
20	    https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/blob/dbf78f67/src/werkzeug/wsgi.py#L828
21	    """

23	    def __init__(self, stream, limit):
24	        self._read = stream.read
25	        self._readline = stream.readline
26	        self._pos = 0
27	        self.limit = limit
28	
29	    def read(self, size=-1, /):
30	        _pos = self._pos
31	        limit = self.limit
32	        if _pos >= limit:
33	            return b""
34	        if size == -1 or size is None:
35	            size = limit - _pos
36	        else:
37	            size = min(size, limit - _pos)
38	        data = self._read(size)
39	        self._pos += len(data)
40	        return data

42	    def readline(self, size=-1, /):
43	        _pos = self._pos
44	        limit = self.limit
45	        if _pos >= limit:
46	            return b""
47	        if size == -1 or size is None:
48	            size = limit - _pos
49	        else:
50	            size = min(size, limit - _pos)
51	        line = self._readline(size)
52	        self._pos += len(line)
53	        return line

173	        """

176	        """
177	        # We try three options, in order of decreasing preference.
178	        if settings.USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST and ("HTTP_X_FORWARDED_HOST" in self.META):
179	            host = self.META["HTTP_X_FORWARDED_HOST"]

268	        to an RFC 3987 compliant URI and return it. If location is relative or
269	        is scheme-relative (i.e., ``//example.com/``), urljoin() it to a base
270	        URL constructed from the request variables.
271	        """
272	        if location is None:
273	            # Make it an absolute url (but schemeless and domainless) for the
274	            # edge case that the path starts with '//'.
275	            location = "//%s" % self.get_full_path()

278	            location = str(location)
279	        bits = urlsplit(location)
280	        if not (bits.scheme and bits.netloc):
281	            # Handle the simple, most common case. If the location is absolute
282	            # and a scheme or host (netloc) isn't provided, skip an expensive
283	            # urljoin() as long as no path segments are '.' or '..'.

288	                and "/./" not in bits.path

292	                # schema and netloc from the current request. Strip the double
293	                # slashes and continue as if it wasn't specified.

297	                # allows the provided location to apply query strings to the
298	                # base path.
299	                location = urljoin(self._current_scheme_host + self.path, location)

106	    def adapt_method_mode(
107	        self,
108	        is_async,
109	        method,
110	        method_is_async=None,
111	        debug=False,
112	        name=None,
113	    ):
114	        """
115	        Adapt a method to be in the correct "mode":
116	        - If is_async is False:
117	          - Synchronous methods are left alone
118	          - Asynchronous methods are wrapped with async_to_sync
119	        - If is_async is True:
120	          - Synchronous methods are wrapped with sync_to_async()
121	          - Asynchronous methods are left alone
122	        """
123	        if method_is_async is None:
124	            method_is_async = iscoroutinefunction(method)
125	        if debug and not name:
126	            name = name or "method %s()" % method.__qualname__
127	        if is_async:
128	            if not method_is_async:
129	                if debug:
130	                    logger.debug("Synchronous handler adapted for %s.", name)
131	                return sync_to_async(method, thread_sensitive=True)
132	        elif method_is_async:
133	            if debug:
134	                logger.debug("Asynchronous handler adapted for %s.", name)
135	            return async_to_sync(method)

302	    def resolve_request(self, request):
303	        """
304	        Retrieve/set the urlconf for the request. Return the view resolved,
305	        with its args and kwargs.
306	        """
307	        # Work out the resolver.
308	        if hasattr(request, "urlconf"):
309	            urlconf = request.urlconf
310	            set_urlconf(urlconf)
311	            resolver = get_resolver(urlconf)
312	        else:
313	            resolver = get_resolver()
314	        # Resolve the view, and assign the match object back to the request.
315	        resolver_match = resolver.resolve(request.path_info)
316	        request.resolver_match = resolver_match
317	        return resolver_match

319	    def check_response(self, response, callback, name=None):
320	        """
321	        Raise an error if the view returned None or an uncalled coroutine.
322	        """
323	        if not (response is None or asyncio.iscoroutine(response)):
324	            return
325	        if not name:
326	            if isinstance(callback, types.FunctionType):  # FBV
327	                name = "The view %s.%s" % (callback.__module__, callback.__name__)
328	            else:  # CBV
329	                name = "The view %s.%s.__call__" % (
330	                    callback.__module__,
331	                    callback.__class__.__name__,
332	                )
333	        if response is None:
334	            raise ValueError(
335	                "%s didn't return an HttpResponse object. It returned None "
336	                "instead." % name

340	                "%s didn't return an HttpResponse object. It returned an "
341	                "unawaited coroutine instead. You may need to add an 'await' "

348	        non_atomic_requests = getattr(view, "_non_atomic_requests", set())
349	        for alias, settings_dict in connections.settings.items():
350	            if settings_dict["ATOMIC_REQUESTS"] and alias not in non_atomic_requests:

25	def convert_exception_to_response(get_response):
26	    """
27	    Wrap the given get_response callable in exception-to-response conversion.
28	
29	    All exceptions will be converted. All known 4xx exceptions (Http404,
30	    PermissionDenied, MultiPartParserError, SuspiciousOperation) will be
31	    converted to the appropriate response, and all other exceptions will be
32	    converted to 500 responses.
33

35	    no middleware leaks an exception and that the next middleware in the stack
36	    can rely on getting a response instead of an exception.
37	    """
38	    if iscoroutinefunction(get_response):
39	
40	        @wraps(get_response)
41	        async def inner(request):
42	            try:
43	                response = await get_response(request)
44	            except Exception as exc:
45	                response = await sync_to_async(
46	                    response_for_exception, thread_sensitive=False
47	                )(request, exc)
48	            return response
49	
50	        return inner
51	    else:
52	
53	        @wraps(get_response)
54	        def inner(request):
55	            try:
56	                response = get_response(request)
57	            except Exception as exc:
58	                response = response_for_exception(request, exc)
59	            return response
60	
61	        return inner

163	def get_exception_response(request, resolver, status_code, exception):
164	    try:

166	        response = callback(request, exception=exception)

174	def handle_uncaught_exception(request, resolver, exc_info):
175	    """
176	    Processing for any otherwise uncaught exceptions (those that will
177	    generate HTTP 500 responses).
178	    """
179	    if settings.DEBUG_PROPAGATE_EXCEPTIONS:
180	        raise
181	
182	    if settings.DEBUG:
183	        return debug.technical_500_response(request, *exc_info)
184	
185	    # Return an HttpResponse that displays a friendly error message.
186	    callback = resolver.resolve_error_handler(500)
187	    return callback(request)

25	def warn_about_external_use(
26	    message,
27	    category,
28	    *,
29	    skip_name_prefixes=None,
30	    skip_frames=0,
31	    internal_modules=None,
32	):
33	    """Issue a warning when a deprecated feature is used outside of Django.
34	
35	    Skip the warning when called from within Django, to avoid cascading
36	    warnings when one deprecated feature is implemented on top of another.
37	
38	    Examine the stack to determine the "effective caller" (the code using the
39	    deprecated feature). By default, this is the third frame from the top
40	    (ignoring this helper plus the call site).
41	
42	    Provide `skip_name_prefixes` (a string or tuple of strings) to skip
43	    additional frames by prefix-matching each frame's fully qualified name
44	    (dotted module path plus qualname). `skip_name_prefixes` can be used to
45	    skip specific functions, all methods in a class, or everything in a module.
46	    Skipping stops at the first non-matching frame. Useful when a shared helper
47	    is called through multiple paths of varying depth with a common prefix.
48	
49	    Provide `skip_frames` (an integer) to skip a fixed number of additional
50	    frames (e.g., exactly one decorator or shared helper function). If both
51	    options are provided, `skip_name_prefixes` is applied first.
52	
53	    Provide `internal_modules` (a tuple of names, defaulting to ("django",)) to
54	    customize what counts as "internal". A frame is internal when its fully
55	    qualified name starts with one of these names followed by a dot.
56	
57	    The warning is issued only if the effective caller (the first non-skipped
58	    frame) is outside Django, attributed to that frame. If all frames are
59	    skipped, it falls back to the base of the stack.
60	
61	    Note: To unconditionally issue a warning identifying the first caller
62	    outside Django as its source, don't use this function. Instead, use::
63	
64	        warnings.warn(..., skip_file_prefixes=django_file_prefixes())
65	
66	    to avoid unnecessary stack inspection overhead.
67	    """
68	
69	    if internal_modules is None:
70	        internal_modules = ("django",)
71	    if not isinstance(internal_modules, tuple):
72	        raise TypeError("internal_modules must be a tuple of module names")
73	    internal_prefixes = tuple(f"{mod}." for mod in internal_modules)
74	
75	    def get_fq_name(frame):
76	        mod_name = frame.f_globals.get("__name__", "__main__")
77	        return f"{mod_name}.{frame.f_code.co_qualname}"
78	
79	    def back(frame, level):
80	        if frame is not None:
81	            return frame.f_back, level + 1
82	        return None, level
83	
84	    frame, level = inspect.currentframe(), 0
85	    try:
86	        # Back two frames: ignore warn_about_external_use() and its caller.
87	        frame, level = back(*back(frame, level))
88	
89	        if skip_name_prefixes is not None:
90	            while frame and get_fq_name(frame).startswith(skip_name_prefixes):
91	                frame, level = back(frame, level)
92	
93	        for _ in range(skip_frames):
94	            frame, level = back(frame, level)
95	
96	        is_internal = frame and get_fq_name(frame).startswith(internal_prefixes)
97	    finally:
98	        del frame
99	
100	    if not is_internal:
101	        warnings.warn(message, category=category, stacklevel=level + 1)

104	class warn_about_renamed_method:
105	    def __init__(
106	        self, class_name, old_method_name, new_method_name, deprecation_warning
107	    ):
108	        self.class_name = class_name
109	        self.old_method_name = old_method_name
110	        self.new_method_name = new_method_name
111	        self.deprecation_warning = deprecation_warning
112	
113	    def __call__(self, f):
114	        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
115	            warnings.warn(
116	                "`%s.%s` is deprecated, use `%s` instead."
117	                % (self.class_name, self.old_method_name, self.new_method_name),
118	                self.deprecation_warning,
119	                2,
120	            )
121	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
122	
123	        return wrapper

126	class RenameMethodsBase(type):
127	    """
128	    Handles the deprecation paths when renaming a method.
129	
130	    It does the following:
131	        1) Define the new method if missing and complain about it.
132	        2) Define the old method if missing.
133	        3) Complain whenever an old method is called.
134	
135	    See #15363 for more details.
136	    """
137	
138	    renamed_methods = ()
139	
140	    def __new__(cls, name, bases, attrs):
141	        new_class = super().__new__(cls, name, bases, attrs)
142	
143	        for base in inspect.getmro(new_class):
144	            class_name = base.__name__
145	            for renamed_method in cls.renamed_methods:
146	                old_method_name = renamed_method[0]
147	                old_method = base.__dict__.get(old_method_name)
148	                new_method_name = renamed_method[1]
149	                new_method = base.__dict__.get(new_method_name)
150	                deprecation_warning = renamed_method[2]
151	                wrapper = warn_about_renamed_method(class_name, *renamed_method)
152	
153	                # Define the new method if missing and complain about it
154	                if not new_method and old_method:
155	                    warnings.warn(
156	                        "`%s.%s` method should be renamed `%s`."
157	                        % (class_name, old_method_name, new_method_name),
158	                        deprecation_warning,
159	                        2,
160	                    )
161	                    setattr(base, new_method_name, old_method)
162	                    setattr(base, old_method_name, wrapper(old_method))
163	
164	                # Define the old method as a wrapped call to the new method.
165	                if not old_method and new_method:
166	                    setattr(base, old_method_name, wrapper(new_method))
167	
168	        return new_class

171	def deprecate_posargs(deprecation_warning, remappable_names, /):
172	    """
173	    Function/method decorator to deprecate some or all positional arguments.
174	
175	    The decorated function will map any positional arguments after the ``*`` to
176	    the corresponding keyword arguments and issue a deprecation warning.
177	
178	    The decorator takes two arguments: a RemovedInDjangoXXWarning warning
179	    category and a list of parameter names that have been changed from
180	    positional-or-keyword to keyword-only, in their original positional order.
181	
182	    Works on both functions and methods. To apply to a class constructor,
183	    decorate its __init__() method. To apply to a staticmethod or classmethod,
184	    use @deprecate_posargs after @staticmethod or @classmethod.
185	
186	    Example: to deprecate passing option1 or option2 as posargs, change::
187	
188	        def some_func(request, option1, option2=True):
189	            ...
190	
191	    to::
192	
193	        @deprecate_posargs(RemovedInDjangoXXWarning, ["option1", "option2"])
194	        def some_func(request, *, option1, option2=True):
195	            ...
196	
197	    After the deprecation period, remove the decorator (but keep the ``*``)::
198	
199	        def some_func(request, *, option1, option2=True):
200	            ...

234	        if isinstance(func, staticmethod):

238	        num_by_kind = Counter(param.kind for param in params.values())
239

241	            raise TypeError(
242	                "@deprecate_posargs() cannot be used with variable positional `*args`."
243	            )

248	        )
249	        num_keyword_only_params = num_by_kind[inspect.Parameter.KEYWORD_ONLY]
250	        if num_keyword_only_params < 1:
251	            raise TypeError(
252	                "@deprecate_posargs() requires at least one keyword-only parameter "
253	                "(after a `*` entry in the parameters list)."

258	        ):

261	                "keyword-only parameters."

292	            # actually _being remapped_ in this particular call.

254	    For example, 'foo/<int:pk>' returns '^foo\\/(?P<pk>[0-9]+)'

257	    parts = ["^"]

265	            )

267	        # matches something).

269	        if not parameter.isidentifier():

271	                f"URL route {route!r} uses parameter name {parameter!r} which "
272	                "isn't a valid Python identifier."
273	            )
274	        try:
275	            converter = all_converters[raw_converter]
276	        except KeyError as e:
277	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
278	                f"URL route {route!r} uses invalid converter {raw_converter!r}."
279	            ) from e
280	        converters[parameter] = converter
281	
282	        start, end = match_.span()
283	        parts.append(re.escape(route[previous_end:start]))
284	        previous_end = end
285	        parts.append(f"(?P<{parameter}>{converter.regex})")
286	
287	    parts.append(re.escape(route[previous_end:]))
288	    if is_endpoint:

545	    def _populate(self):

547	        # infinite recursion. Concurrent threads may call this at the same
548	        # time and will need to continue, so set 'populating' on a
549	        # thread-local variable.
550	        if getattr(self._local, "populating", False):
551	            return
552	        try:
553	            self._local.populating = True

580	                                url_pattern.pattern.converters,
581	                            ),
582	                        )

587	                            url_pattern.namespace
588	                        )

591	                        for name in url_pattern.reverse_dict:

617	                            sub_pattern.pattern.converters.update(current_converters)
618	                            namespaces[namespace] = (p_pattern + prefix, sub_pattern)

1	import re
2	
3	from django.conf import settings
4	from django.http import HttpResponsePermanentRedirect
5	from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin
6	
7	
8	class SecurityMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
9	    def __init__(self, get_response):
10	        super().__init__(get_response)
11	        self.sts_seconds = settings.SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS
12	        self.sts_include_subdomains = settings.SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS
13	        self.sts_preload = settings.SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD
14	        self.content_type_nosniff = settings.SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF
15	        self.redirect = settings.SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT
16	        self.redirect_host = settings.SECURE_SSL_HOST
17	        self.redirect_exempt = [re.compile(r) for r in settings.SECURE_REDIRECT_EXEMPT]
18	        self.referrer_policy = settings.SECURE_REFERRER_POLICY
19	        self.cross_origin_opener_policy = settings.SECURE_CROSS_ORIGIN_OPENER_POLICY
20	
21	    def process_request(self, request):
22	        path = request.path.lstrip("/")
23	        if (
24	            self.redirect
25	            and not request.is_secure()
26	            and not any(pattern.search(path) for pattern in self.redirect_exempt)
27	        ):
28	            host = self.redirect_host or request.get_host()
29	            return HttpResponsePermanentRedirect(
30	                "https://%s%s" % (host, request.get_full_path())
31	            )
32	
33	    def process_response(self, request, response):
34	        if (
35	            self.sts_seconds
36	            and request.is_secure()
37	            and "Strict-Transport-Security" not in response
38	        ):
39	            sts_header = "max-age=%s" % self.sts_seconds
40	            if self.sts_include_subdomains:
41	                sts_header += "; includeSubDomains"
42	            if self.sts_preload:
43	                sts_header += "; preload"
44	            response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = sts_header
45	
46	        if self.content_type_nosniff:
47	            response.headers.setdefault("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff")
48	
49	        if self.referrer_policy:
50	            # Support a comma-separated string or iterable of values to allow
51	            # fallback.
52	            response.headers.setdefault(
53	                "Referrer-Policy",
54	                ",".join(
55	                    [v.strip() for v in self.referrer_policy.split(",")]
56	                    if isinstance(self.referrer_policy, str)
57	                    else self.referrer_policy
58	                ),
59	            )
60	
61	        if self.cross_origin_opener_policy:
62	            response.setdefault(
63	                "Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy",
64	                self.cross_origin_opener_policy,
65	            )
66	        return response

22	class ContextMixin:

34	        return kwargs

37	class View:
38	    """
39	    Intentionally simple parent class for all views. Only implements
40	    dispatch-by-method and simple sanity checking.
41	    """
42	
43	    http_method_names = [
44	        "get",
45	        "post",
46	        "put",
47	        "patch",
48	        "delete",
49	        "head",
50	        "options",
51	        "trace",
52	    ]
53	
54	    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
55	        """
56	        Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra
57	        keyword arguments, and other things.
58	        """
59	        # Go through keyword arguments, and either save their values to our
60	        # instance, or raise an error.
61	        for key, value in kwargs.items():
62	            setattr(self, key, value)
63	
64	    @classproperty
65	    def view_is_async(cls):
66	        handlers = [
67	            getattr(cls, method)
68	            for method in cls.http_method_names
69	            if (method != "options" and hasattr(cls, method))
70	        ]
71	        if not handlers:
72	            return False
73	        is_async = iscoroutinefunction(handlers[0])
74	        if not all(iscoroutinefunction(h) == is_async for h in handlers[1:]):
75	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
76	                f"{cls.__qualname__} HTTP handlers must either be all sync or all "
77	                "async."
78	            )
79	        return is_async
80	
81	    @classonlymethod
82	    def as_view(cls, **initkwargs):
83	        """Main entry point for a request-response process."""
84	        for key in initkwargs:
85	            if key in cls.http_method_names:
86	                raise TypeError(
87	                    "The method name %s is not accepted as a keyword argument "
88	                    "to %s()." % (key, cls.__name__)
89	                )
90	            if not hasattr(cls, key):
91	                raise TypeError(
92	                    "%s() received an invalid keyword %r. as_view "
93	                    "only accepts arguments that are already "
94	                    "attributes of the class." % (cls.__name__, key)
95	                )
96	
97	        def view(request, *args, **kwargs):
98	            self = cls(**initkwargs)
99	            self.setup(request, *args, **kwargs)
100	            if not hasattr(self, "request"):
101	                raise AttributeError(
102	                    "%s instance has no 'request' attribute. Did you override "
103	                    "setup() and forget to call super()?" % cls.__name__
104	                )
105	            return self.dispatch(request, *args, **kwargs)
106	
107	        view.view_class = cls
108	        view.view_initkwargs = initkwargs
109	
110	        # __name__ and __qualname__ are intentionally left unchanged as
111	        # view_class should be used to robustly determine the name of the view
112	        # instead.
113	        view.__doc__ = cls.__doc__
114	        view.__module__ = cls.__module__
115	        view.__annotations__ = cls.dispatch.__annotations__
116	        # Copy possible attributes set by decorators, e.g. @csrf_exempt, from
117	        # the dispatch method.
118	        view.__dict__.update(cls.dispatch.__dict__)
119	
120	        # Mark the callback if the view class is async.
121	        if cls.view_is_async:
122	            markcoroutinefunction(view)
123	
124	        return view

126	    def setup(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
127	        """Initialize attributes shared by all view methods."""
128	        if hasattr(self, "get") and not hasattr(self, "head"):
129	            self.head = self.get
130	        self.request = request
131	        self.args = args
132	        self.kwargs = kwargs
133	
134	    def dispatch(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
135	        # Try to dispatch to the right method; if a method doesn't exist,
136	        # defer to the error handler. Also defer to the error handler if the
137	        # request method isn't on the approved list.
138	        method = request.method.lower()
139	        if method in self.http_method_names:
140	            handler = getattr(self, method, self.http_method_not_allowed)
141	        else:
142	            handler = self.http_method_not_allowed
143	        return handler(request, *args, **kwargs)

145	    def http_method_not_allowed(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
146	        response = HttpResponseNotAllowed(self._allowed_methods())

153	        )

155	        if self.view_is_async:
156	
157	            async def func():

160	            return func()

162	            return response
163	
164	    def options(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
165	        """Handle responding to requests for the OPTIONS HTTP verb."""

168	        response.headers["Content-Length"] = "0"

172	            async def func():

174	
175	            return func()

179	    def _allowed_methods(self):

56	        """
57	        try:

61	            elif isinstance(value, bytes):

63	                value = value.decode(charset)
64	            else:

66	                # Ensure string is valid in given charset.
67	                value.encode(charset)

71	                )

74	            # don't know what type that value was, or if it contains newlines,
75	            # which we may need to check for before sending it to be
76	            # encoded for multiple character sets.
77	            if (isinstance(value, bytes) and (b"\n" in value or b"\r" in value)) or (
78	                isinstance(value, str) and ("\n" in value or "\r" in value)
79	            ):
80	                raise BadHeaderError(
81	                    f"Header values can't contain newlines (got {value!r})"
82	                ) from e

12	class SessionMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
13	    def __init__(self, get_response):
14	        super().__init__(get_response)
15	        engine = import_module(settings.SESSION_ENGINE)
16	        self.SessionStore = engine.SessionStore
17	
18	    def process_request(self, request):
19	        session_key = request.COOKIES.get(settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME)
20	        request.session = self.SessionStore(session_key)
21	
22	    def process_response(self, request, response):
23	        """
24	        If request.session was modified, or if the configuration is to save the
25	        session every time, save the changes and set a session cookie or delete
26	        the session cookie if the session has been emptied.
27	        """
28	        try:
29	            accessed = request.session.accessed
30	            modified = request.session.modified
31	            empty = request.session.is_empty()
32	        except AttributeError:
33	            return response
34	        # First check if we need to delete this cookie.
35	        # The session should be deleted only if the session is entirely empty.
36	        if settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME in request.COOKIES and empty:
37	            response.delete_cookie(
38	                settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,
39	                path=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_PATH,
40	                domain=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN,
41	                samesite=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE,
42	            )
43	            need_vary_cookie = True
44	        else:
45	            # If the session was accessed, it must be varied on, regardless of
46	            # whether it was modified or will be saved.
47	            need_vary_cookie = accessed
48	            if (modified or settings.SESSION_SAVE_EVERY_REQUEST) and not empty:
49	                if request.session.get_expire_at_browser_close():
50	                    max_age = None
51	                    expires = None
52	                else:
53	                    max_age = request.session.get_expiry_age()
54	                    expires_time = time.time() + max_age
55	                    expires = http_date(expires_time)
56	                # Save the session data and refresh the client cookie.
57	                # Skip session save for 5xx responses.
58	                if response.status_code < 500:
59	                    try:
60	                        request.session.save()
61	                    except UpdateError:
62	                        raise SessionInterrupted(
63	                            "The request's session was deleted before the "
64	                            "request completed. The user may have logged "
65	                            "out in a concurrent request, for example."
66	                        )
67	                    response.set_cookie(
68	                        settings.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,
69	                        request.session.session_key,
70	                        max_age=max_age,
71	                        expires=expires,
72	                        domain=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN,
73	                        path=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_PATH,
74	                        secure=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE or None,
75	                        httponly=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY or None,
76	                        samesite=settings.SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE,
77	                    )
78	                    # With a session cookie set, it must be varied on.
79	                    need_vary_cookie = True
80	        if need_vary_cookie:
81	            patch_vary_headers(response, ("Cookie",))
82	        return response

167	class RawModelIterable(BaseIterable):
168	    """
169	    Iterable that yields a model instance for each row from a raw queryset.
170	    """
171	
172	    def __iter__(self):
173	        # Cache some things for performance reasons outside the loop.
174	        db = self.queryset.db
175	        query = self.queryset.query
176	        connection = connections[db]
177	        compiler = connection.ops.compiler("SQLCompiler")(query, connection, db)
178	        query_iterator = iter(query)
179	
180	        try:
181	            (
182	                model_init_names,
183	                model_init_pos,
184	                annotation_fields,
185	            ) = self.queryset.resolve_model_init_order()
186	            model_cls = self.queryset.model
187	            if any(
188	                f.attname not in model_init_names for f in model_cls._meta.pk_fields
189	            ):
190	                raise exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist(
191	                    "Raw query must include the primary key"
192	                )
193	            fields = [self.queryset.model_fields.get(c) for c in self.queryset.columns]
194	            cols = [f.get_col(f.model._meta.db_table) if f else None for f in fields]
195	            converters = compiler.get_converters(cols)
196	            if converters:
197	                query_iterator = compiler.apply_converters(query_iterator, converters)
198	            if compiler.has_composite_fields(cols):
199	                query_iterator = compiler.composite_fields_to_tuples(
200	                    query_iterator, cols
201	                )
202	            fetch_mode = self.queryset._fetch_mode
203	            peers = []
204	            for values in query_iterator:
205	                # Associate fields to values
206	                model_init_values = [values[pos] for pos in model_init_pos]
207	                instance = model_cls.from_db(
208	                    db, model_init_names, model_init_values, fetch_mode=fetch_mode
209	                )
210	                if fetch_mode.track_peers:
211	                    peers.append(weak_ref(instance))
212	                    instance._state.peers = peers
213	                if annotation_fields:
214	                    for column, pos in annotation_fields:
215	                        setattr(instance, column, values[pos])
216	                yield instance
217	        finally:
218	            # Done iterating the Query. If it has its own cursor, close it.
219	            if hasattr(query, "cursor") and query.cursor:
220	                query.cursor.close()

223	class ValuesIterable(BaseIterable):
224	    """
225	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values() that yields a dict for each row.
226	    """
227	
228	    def __iter__(self):
229	        queryset = self.queryset
230	        query = queryset.query
231	        compiler = query.get_compiler(queryset.db)
232	
233	        if query.selected:
234	            names = list(query.selected)
235	        else:
236	            # extra(select=...) cols are always at the start of the row.
237	            names = [
238	                *query.extra_select,
239	                *query.values_select,
240	                *query.annotation_select,
241	            ]
242	        indexes = range(len(names))
243	        for row in compiler.results_iter(
244	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size
245	        ):
246	            yield {names[i]: row[i] for i in indexes}

420	    def __iter__(self):
421	        """
422	        The queryset iterator protocol uses three nested iterators in the
423	        default case:
424	            1. sql.compiler.execute_sql()
425	               - Returns 100 rows at time (constants.GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE)
426	                 using cursor.fetchmany(). This part is responsible for
427	                 doing some column masking, and returning the rows in chunks.
428	            2. sql.compiler.results_iter()
429	               - Returns one row at time. At this point the rows are still just
430	                 tuples. In some cases the return values are converted to
431	                 Python values at this location.
432	            3. self.iterator()
433	               - Responsible for turning the rows into model objects.
434	        """
435	        self._fetch_all()
436	        return iter(self._result_cache)

438	    def __aiter__(self):
439	        # Remember, __aiter__ itself is synchronous, it's the thing it returns
440	        # that is async!
441	        async def generator():
442	            await sync_to_async(self._fetch_all)()
443	            for item in self._result_cache:
444	                yield item
445	
446	        return generator()

448	    def __bool__(self):

452	    def __getitem__(self, k):
453	        """Retrieve an item or slice from the set of results."""
454	        if not isinstance(k, (int, slice)):
455	            raise TypeError(
456	                "QuerySet indices must be integers or slices, not %s."
457	                % type(k).__name__
458	            )
459	        if (isinstance(k, int) and k < 0) or (
460	            isinstance(k, slice)
461	            and (
462	                (k.start is not None and k.start < 0)
463	                or (k.stop is not None and k.stop < 0)
464	            )
465	        ):
466	            raise ValueError("Negative indexing is not supported.")
467	
468	        if self._result_cache is not None:
469	            return self._result_cache[k]
470	
471	        if isinstance(k, slice):
472	            qs = self._chain()
473	            if k.start is not None:
474	                start = int(k.start)
475	            else:
476	                start = None
477	            if k.stop is not None:
478	                stop = int(k.stop)
479	            else:
480	                stop = None
481	            qs.query.set_limits(start, stop)
482	            return list(qs)[:: k.step] if k.step else qs
483	
484	        qs = self._chain()
485	        qs.query.set_limits(k, k + 1)
486	        qs._fetch_all()
487	        return qs._result_cache[0]

492	    def __and__(self, other):
493	        self._check_operator_queryset(other, "&")
494	        self._merge_sanity_check(other)
495	        if isinstance(other, EmptyQuerySet):
496	            return other
497	        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):
498	            return self
499	        combined = self._chain()
500	        combined._merge_known_related_objects(other)
501	        combined.query.combine(other.query, sql.AND)
502	        return combined

504	    def __or__(self, other):
505	        self._check_operator_queryset(other, "|")
506	        self._merge_sanity_check(other)
507	        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):
508	            return other
509	        if isinstance(other, EmptyQuerySet):
510	            return self
511	        query = (
512	            self
513	            if self.query.can_filter()
514	            else self.model._base_manager.filter(pk__in=self.values("pk"))
515	        )
516	        combined = query._chain()
517	        combined._merge_known_related_objects(other)
518	        if not other.query.can_filter():
519	            other = other.model._base_manager.filter(pk__in=other.values("pk"))
520	        combined.query.combine(other.query, sql.OR)
521	        return combined

523	    def __xor__(self, other):
524	        self._check_operator_queryset(other, "^")
525	        self._merge_sanity_check(other)
526	        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):
527	            return other
528	        if isinstance(other, EmptyQuerySet):
529	            return self
530	        query = (
531	            self
532	            if self.query.can_filter()
533	            else self.model._base_manager.filter(pk__in=self.values("pk"))
534	        )
535	        combined = query._chain()
536	        combined._merge_known_related_objects(other)
537	        if not other.query.can_filter():
538	            other = other.model._base_manager.filter(pk__in=other.values("pk"))
539	        combined.query.combine(other.query, sql.XOR)
540	        return combined

546	    def _iterator(self, use_chunked_fetch, chunk_size):
547	        iterable = self._iterable_class(
548	            self,
549	            chunked_fetch=use_chunked_fetch,
550	            chunk_size=chunk_size or 2000,
551	        )
552	        if not self._prefetch_related_lookups or chunk_size is None:
553	            yield from iterable
554	            return
555	
556	        iterator = iter(iterable)
557	        while results := list(islice(iterator, chunk_size)):
558	            prefetch_related_objects(results, *self._prefetch_related_lookups)
559	            yield from results

561	    def iterator(self, chunk_size=None):
562	        """
563	        An iterator over the results from applying this QuerySet to the
564	        database. chunk_size must be provided for QuerySets that prefetch
565	        related objects. Otherwise, a default chunk_size of 2000 is supplied.
566	        """
567	        if chunk_size is None:
568	            if self._prefetch_related_lookups:
569	                raise ValueError(
570	                    "chunk_size must be provided when using QuerySet.iterator() after "
571	                    "prefetch_related()."
572	                )
573	        elif chunk_size <= 0:
574	            raise ValueError("Chunk size must be strictly positive.")
575	        use_chunked_fetch = not connections[self.db].settings_dict.get(
576	            "DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS"
577	        )
578	        return self._iterator(use_chunked_fetch, chunk_size)
579	
580	    async def aiterator(self, chunk_size=None):
581	        """
582	        An asynchronous iterator over the results from applying this QuerySet
583	        to the database.
584	        """
585	        if chunk_size is None:
586	            if self._prefetch_related_lookups:
587	                # RemovedInDjango71Warning: Replace the warning with:
588	                # raise ValueError(
589	                #     "chunk_size must be provided when using "
590	                #     "QuerySet.aiterator() after prefetch_related()."
591	                # )
592	                warnings.warn(
593	                    "Using QuerySet.aiterator() after prefetch_related() without "
594	                    "providing a chunk_size is deprecated and will raise a "
595	                    "ValueError in Django 7.1.",
596	                    category=RemovedInDjango71Warning,
597	                    skip_file_prefixes=django_file_prefixes(),
598	                )
599	                # RemovedInDjango71Warning: When the deprecation ends, remove.
600	                chunk_size = 2000
601	        elif chunk_size <= 0:
602	            raise ValueError("Chunk size must be strictly positive.")
603	        use_chunked_fetch = not connections[self.db].settings_dict.get(
604	            "DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS"
605	        )
606	        iterable = self._iterable_class(
607	            self,
608	            chunked_fetch=use_chunked_fetch,
609	            chunk_size=chunk_size or 2000,
610	        )
611	        if self._prefetch_related_lookups:
612	            results = []
613	
614	            async for item in iterable:
615	                results.append(item)
616	                if len(results) >= chunk_size:
617	                    await aprefetch_related_objects(
618	                        results, *self._prefetch_related_lookups
619	                    )
620	                    for result in results:
621	                        yield result
622	                    results.clear()
623	
624	            if results:
625	                await aprefetch_related_objects(
626	                    results, *self._prefetch_related_lookups
627	                )
628	                for result in results:
629	                    yield result
630	        else:
631	            async for item in iterable:
632	                yield item

634	    def aggregate(self, *args, **kwargs):
635	        """
636	        Return a dictionary containing the calculations (aggregation)
637	        over the current queryset.
638	
639	        If args is present the expression is passed as a kwarg using
640	        the Aggregate object's default alias.
641	        """
642	        if self.query.distinct_fields:
643	            raise NotImplementedError("aggregate() + distinct(fields) not implemented.")
644	        self._validate_values_are_expressions(
645	            (*args, *kwargs.values()), method_name="aggregate"
646	        )
647	        for arg in args:
648	            # The default_alias property raises TypeError if default_alias
649	            # can't be set automatically or AttributeError if it isn't an
650	            # attribute.
651	            try:
652	                arg.default_alias
653	            except (AttributeError, TypeError):
654	                raise TypeError("Complex aggregates require an alias")
655	            kwargs[arg.default_alias] = arg
656	
657	        return self.query.chain().get_aggregation(self.db, kwargs)

231	class RedirectView(View):
232	    """Provide a redirect on any GET request."""
233	
234	    permanent = False
235	    url = None
236	    pattern_name = None
237	    query_string = False

240	    def get_redirect_url(self, *args, **kwargs):
241	        """
242	        Return the URL redirect to. Keyword arguments from the URL pattern
243	        match generating the redirect request are provided as kwargs to this
244	        method.
245	        """
246	        if self.url:
247	            url = self.url % kwargs
248	        elif self.pattern_name:
249	            url = reverse(self.pattern_name, args=args, kwargs=kwargs)
250	        else:
251	            return None
252	
253	        args = self.request.META.get("QUERY_STRING", "")
254	        if args and self.query_string:
255	            if urlparse(url).query:
256	                url = f"{url}&{args}"
257	            else:
258	                url = f"{url}?{args}"
259	        return url
260	
261	    def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
262	        url = self.get_redirect_url(*args, **kwargs)
263	        if url:
264	            if self.permanent:
265	                return HttpResponsePermanentRedirect(
266	                    url, preserve_request=self.preserve_request
267	                )
268	            else:
269	                return HttpResponseRedirect(url, preserve_request=self.preserve_request)
270	        else:
271	            response = HttpResponseGone()
272	            log_response("Gone: %s", request.path, response=response, request=request)
273	            return response

238	        ``expires`` can be:

91	        Return the first DjangoTemplates backend that's configured, or raise
92	        ImproperlyConfigured if none are configured.
93	
94	        This is required for preserving historical APIs that rely on a
95	        globally available, implicitly configured engine such as:
96	
97	        >>> from django.template import Context, Template
98	        >>> template = Template("Hello {{ name }}!")
99	        >>> context = Context({'name': "world"})
100	        >>> template.render(context)
101	        'Hello world!'

103	        # Since Engine is imported in django.template and since
104	        # DjangoTemplates is a wrapper around this Engine class,
105	        # local imports are required to avoid import loops.
106	        from django.template import engines
107	        from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
108	
109	        for engine in engines.all():
110	            if isinstance(engine, DjangoTemplates):
111	                return engine.engine
112	        raise ImproperlyConfigured("No DjangoTemplates backend is configured.")

172	    def get_template(self, template_name):
173	        """
174	        Return a compiled Template object for the given template name,
175	        handling template inheritance recursively.
176	        """
177	        original_name = template_name
178	        try:
179	            template_name, _, partial_name = template_name.partition("#")
180	        except AttributeError:
181	            raise TemplateDoesNotExist(original_name)
182	
183	        if not template_name:
184	            raise TemplateDoesNotExist(original_name)

187	        if not hasattr(template, "render"):

189	            template = Template(template, origin, template_name, engine=self)

191	        if not partial_name:

194	        extra_data = getattr(template, "extra_data", {})
195	        try:
196	            partial = extra_data["partials"][partial_name]
197	        except (KeyError, TypeError):
198	            raise TemplateDoesNotExist(partial_name, tried=[template_name])

203	    def render_to_string(self, template_name, context=None):

206	        For use in Django's test suite.
207	        """
208	        if isinstance(template_name, (list, tuple)):
209	            t = self.select_template(template_name)
210	        else:
211	            t = self.get_template(template_name)
212	        # Django < 1.8 accepted a Context in `context` even though that's
213	        # unintended. Preserve this ability but don't rewrap `context`.
214	        if isinstance(context, Context):
215	            return t.render(context)
216	        else:
217	            return t.render(Context(context, autoescape=self.autoescape))

219	    def select_template(self, template_name_list):
220	        """
221	        Given a list of template names, return the first that can be loaded.
222	        """
223	        if not template_name_list:
224	            raise TemplateDoesNotExist("No template names provided")
225	        not_found = []
226	        for template_name in template_name_list:
227	            try:
228	                return self.get_template(template_name)
229	            except TemplateDoesNotExist as exc:
230	                if exc.args[0] not in not_found:
231	                    not_found.append(exc.args[0])
232	                continue
233	        # If we get here, none of the templates could be loaded
234	        raise TemplateDoesNotExist(", ".join(not_found))

13	class CommonMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
14	    """
15	    "Common" middleware for taking care of some basic operations:
16	
17	        - Forbid access to User-Agents in settings.DISALLOWED_USER_AGENTS
18	
19	        - URL rewriting: Based on the APPEND_SLASH and PREPEND_WWW settings,
20	          append missing slashes and/or prepends missing "www."s.
21	
22	            - If APPEND_SLASH is set and the initial URL doesn't end with a
23	              slash, and it is not found in urlpatterns, form a new URL by
24	              appending a slash at the end. If this new URL is found in
25	              urlpatterns, return an HTTP redirect to this new URL; otherwise
26	              process the initial URL as usual.
27	
28	          This behavior can be customized by subclassing CommonMiddleware and
29	          overriding the response_redirect_class attribute.
30	    """
31	
32	    response_redirect_class = HttpResponsePermanentRedirect
33	
34	    def process_request(self, request):
35	        """
36	        Check for denied User-Agents and rewrite the URL based on
37	        settings.APPEND_SLASH and settings.PREPEND_WWW
38	        """
39	
40	        # Check for denied User-Agents
41	        user_agent = request.META.get("HTTP_USER_AGENT")
42	        if user_agent is not None:
43	            for user_agent_regex in settings.DISALLOWED_USER_AGENTS:
44	                if user_agent_regex.search(user_agent):
45	                    raise PermissionDenied("Forbidden user agent")
46	
47	        # Check for a redirect based on settings.PREPEND_WWW
48	        host = request.get_host()
49	
50	        if settings.PREPEND_WWW and host and not host.startswith("www."):
51	            # Check if we also need to append a slash so we can do it all
52	            # with a single redirect. (This check may be somewhat expensive,
53	            # so we only do it if we already know we're sending a redirect,
54	            # or in process_response if we get a 404.)
55	            if self.should_redirect_with_slash(request):
56	                path = self.get_full_path_with_slash(request)
57	            else:
58	                path = request.get_full_path()
59	
60	            return self.response_redirect_class(f"{request.scheme}://www.{host}{path}")
61	
62	    def should_redirect_with_slash(self, request):
63	        """
64	        Return True if settings.APPEND_SLASH is True and appending a slash to
65	        the request path turns an invalid path into a valid one.
66	        """
67	        if settings.APPEND_SLASH and not request.path_info.endswith("/"):
68	            urlconf = getattr(request, "urlconf", None)
69	            if not is_valid_path(request.path_info, urlconf):
70	                match = is_valid_path("%s/" % request.path_info, urlconf)
71	                if match:
72	                    view = match.func
73	                    return getattr(view, "should_append_slash", True)
74	        return False
75	
76	    def get_full_path_with_slash(self, request):
77	        """
78	        Return the full path of the request with a trailing slash appended.
79	
80	        Raise a RuntimeError if settings.DEBUG is True and request.method is
81	        DELETE, POST, PUT, or PATCH.
82	        """
83	        new_path = request.get_full_path(force_append_slash=True)
84	        # Prevent construction of scheme relative urls.
85	        new_path = escape_leading_slashes(new_path)
86	        if settings.DEBUG and request.method in ("DELETE", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH"):
87	            raise RuntimeError(
88	                "You called this URL via %(method)s, but the URL doesn't end "
89	                "in a slash and you have APPEND_SLASH set. Django can't "
90	                "redirect to the slash URL while maintaining %(method)s data. "
91	                "Change your form to point to %(url)s (note the trailing "
92	                "slash), or set APPEND_SLASH=False in your Django settings."
93	                % {
94	                    "method": request.method,
95	                    "url": request.get_host() + new_path,
96	                }
97	            )
98	        return new_path
99	
100	    def process_response(self, request, response):
101	        """
102	        When the status code of the response is 404, it may redirect to a path
103	        with an appended slash if should_redirect_with_slash() returns True.
104	        """
105	        # If the given URL is "Not Found", then check if we should redirect to
106	        # a path with a slash appended.
107	        if response.status_code == 404 and self.should_redirect_with_slash(request):
108	            response = self.response_redirect_class(
109	                self.get_full_path_with_slash(request)
110	            )
111	
112	        # Add the Content-Length header to non-streaming responses if not
113	        # already set.
114	        if not response.streaming and not response.has_header("Content-Length"):
115	            response.headers["Content-Length"] = str(len(response.content))
116	
117	        return response

120	class BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
121	    # Set to override the mail.mailers alias used for sending the email.
122	    using = None
123	
124	    def process_response(self, request, response):
125	        """Send broken link emails for relevant 404 NOT FOUND responses."""
126	        if response.status_code == 404 and not settings.DEBUG:
127	            domain = request.get_host()
128	            path = request.get_full_path()
129	            referer = request.META.get("HTTP_REFERER", "")
130	
131	            if not self.is_ignorable_request(request, path, domain, referer):
132	                ua = request.META.get("HTTP_USER_AGENT", "<none>")
133	                ip = request.META.get("REMOTE_ADDR", "<none>")
134	                self.send_mail(
135	                    "Broken %slink on %s"
136	                    % (
137	                        (
138	                            "INTERNAL "
139	                            if self.is_internal_request(domain, referer)
140	                            else ""
141	                        ),
142	                        domain,
143	                    ),
144	                    "Referrer: %s\nRequested URL: %s\nUser agent: %s\n"
145	                    "IP address: %s\n" % (referer, path, ua, ip),
146	                )
147	        return response
148	
149	    def send_mail(self, subject, message, *args, **kwargs):
150	        # RemovedInDjango70Warning.
151	        if not mailers._is_configured:
152	            mail_managers(subject, message, *args, fail_silently=True, **kwargs)
153	            return
154	
155	        try:
156	            mail_managers(subject, message, *args, using=self.using, **kwargs)
157	        except MailerDoesNotExist:
158	            pass
159	
160	    def is_internal_request(self, domain, referer):
161	        """
162	        Return True if the referring URL is the same domain as the current
163	        request.
164	        """
165	        # Different subdomains are treated as different domains.
166	        return bool(re.match("^https?://%s/" % re.escape(domain), referer))
167	
168	    def is_ignorable_request(self, request, uri, domain, referer):
169	        """
170	        Return True if the given request *shouldn't* notify the site managers
171	        according to project settings or in situations outlined by the inline
172	        comments.
173	        """
174	        # The referer is empty.
175	        if not referer:
176	            return True
177	
178	        # APPEND_SLASH is enabled and the referer is equal to the current URL
179	        # without a trailing slash indicating an internal redirect.
180	        if settings.APPEND_SLASH and uri.endswith("/") and referer == uri[:-1]:
181	            return True
182	
183	        # A '?' in referer is identified as a search engine source.
184	        if not self.is_internal_request(domain, referer) and "?" in referer:
185	            return True
186	
187	        # The referer is equal to the current URL, ignoring the scheme (assumed
188	        # to be a poorly implemented bot).
189	        parsed_referer = urlsplit(referer)
190	        if parsed_referer.netloc in ["", domain] and parsed_referer.path == uri:
191	            return True
192	
193	        return any(pattern.search(uri) for pattern in settings.IGNORABLE_404_URLS)
