992	                            raise AttributeError
993	                        current = getattr(current, bit)
994	                    except (TypeError, AttributeError):

1180	def token_kwargs(bits, parser, support_legacy=False):
1181	    """
1182	    Parse token keyword arguments and return a dictionary of the arguments
1183	    retrieved from the ``bits`` token list.
1184	
1185	    `bits` is a list containing the remainder of the token (split by spaces)
1186	    that is to be checked for arguments. Valid arguments are removed from this
1187	    list.
1188	
1189	    `support_legacy` - if True, the legacy format ``1 as foo`` is accepted.
1190	    Otherwise, only the standard ``foo=1`` format is allowed.
1191	
1192	    There is no requirement for all remaining token ``bits`` to be keyword
1193	    arguments, so return the dictionary as soon as an invalid argument format
1194	    is reached.
1195	    """
1196	    if not bits:
1197	        return {}
1198	    match = kwarg_re.match(bits[0])
1199	    kwarg_format = match and match[1]
1200	    if not kwarg_format:
1201	        if not support_legacy:
1202	            return {}
1203	        if len(bits) < 3 or bits[1] != "as":
1204	            return {}
1205	
1206	    kwargs = {}
1207	    while bits:
1208	        if kwarg_format:
1209	            match = kwarg_re.match(bits[0])
1210	            if not match or not match[1]:
1211	                return kwargs
1212	            key, value = match.groups()
1213	            del bits[:1]
1214	        else:
1215	            if len(bits) < 3 or bits[1] != "as":
1216	                return kwargs
1217	            key, value = bits[2], bits[0]
1218	            del bits[:3]
1219	        kwargs[key] = parser.compile_filter(value)
1220	        if bits and not kwarg_format:
1221	            if bits[0] != "and":
1222	                return kwargs
1223	            del bits[:1]
1224	    return kwargs

21	def get_connection(using=None):
22	    """
23	    Get a database connection by name, or the default database connection
24	    if no name is provided. This is a private API.
25	    """
26	    if using is None:
27	        using = DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS
28	    return connections[using]

42	    """Commit a transaction."""

61	    """
62	    Create a savepoint (if supported and required by the backend) inside the
63	    current transaction. Return an identifier for the savepoint that will be
64	    used for the subsequent rollback or commit.

97	def set_rollback(rollback, using=None):
98	    """
99	    Set or unset the "needs rollback" flag -- for *advanced use* only.

101	    When `rollback` is `True`, trigger a rollback when exiting the innermost
102	    enclosing atomic block that has `savepoint=True` (that's the default). Use
103	    this to force a rollback without raising an exception.

114	    """
115	    Internal low-level utility to mark a transaction as "needs rollback" when
116	    an exception is raised while not enforcing the enclosed block to be in a
117	    transaction. This is needed by Model.save() and friends to avoid starting a
118	    transaction when in autocommit mode and a single query is executed.
119	
120	    It's equivalent to:
121	
122	        connection = get_connection(using)
123	        if connection.get_autocommit():
124	            yield
125	        else:
126	            with transaction.atomic(using=using, savepoint=False):
127	                yield
128	
129	    but it uses low-level utilities to avoid performance overhead.
130	    """
131	    try:
132	        yield
133	    except Exception as exc:
134	        connection = get_connection(using)
135	        if connection.in_atomic_block:
136	            connection.needs_rollback = True
137	            connection.rollback_exc = exc
138	        raise

144	    If the current transaction is rolled back, `func` will not be called.
145	    """
146	    get_connection(using).on_commit(func, robust)

154	class Atomic(ContextDecorator):
155	    """
156	    Guarantee the atomic execution of a given block.
157	
158	    An instance can be used either as a decorator or as a context manager.
159	
160	    When it's used as a decorator, __call__ wraps the execution of the
161	    decorated function in the instance itself, used as a context manager.
162	
163	    When it's used as a context manager, __enter__ creates a transaction or a
164	    savepoint, depending on whether a transaction is already in progress, and
165	    __exit__ commits the transaction or releases the savepoint on normal exit,
166	    and rolls back the transaction or to the savepoint on exceptions.
167	
168	    It's possible to disable the creation of savepoints if the goal is to
169	    ensure that some code runs within a transaction without creating overhead.
170	
171	    A stack of savepoint identifiers is maintained as an attribute of the
172	    connection. None denotes the absence of a savepoint.
173	
174	    This allows reentrancy even if the same AtomicWrapper is reused. For
175	    example, it's possible to define `oa = atomic('other')` and use `@oa` or
176	    `with oa:` multiple times.
177	
178	    Since database connections are thread-local, this is thread-safe.
179	
180	    An atomic block can be tagged as durable. In this case, a RuntimeError is
181	    raised if it's nested within another atomic block. This guarantees
182	    that database changes in a durable block are committed to the database when
183	    the block exits without error.
184	
185	    This is a private API.
186	    """
187	
188	    def __init__(self, using, savepoint, durable):
189	        self.using = using
190	        self.savepoint = savepoint
191	        self.durable = durable
192	        self._from_testcase = False
193	
194	    def __enter__(self):
195	        connection = get_connection(self.using)
196	
197	        if (
198	            self.durable
199	            and connection.atomic_blocks
200	            and not connection.atomic_blocks[-1]._from_testcase
201	        ):
202	            raise RuntimeError(
203	                "A durable atomic block cannot be nested within another "
204	                "atomic block."
205	            )
206	        if not connection.in_atomic_block:
207	            # Reset state when entering an outermost atomic block.
208	            connection.commit_on_exit = True
209	            connection.needs_rollback = False
210	            if not connection.get_autocommit():
211	                # Pretend we're already in an atomic block to bypass the code
212	                # that disables autocommit to enter a transaction, and make a
213	                # note to deal with this case in __exit__.
214	                connection.in_atomic_block = True
215	                connection.commit_on_exit = False
216	
217	        if connection.in_atomic_block:
218	            # We're already in a transaction; create a savepoint, unless we
219	            # were told not to or we're already waiting for a rollback. The
220	            # second condition avoids creating useless savepoints and prevents
221	            # overwriting needs_rollback until the rollback is performed.
222	            if self.savepoint and not connection.needs_rollback:
223	                sid = connection.savepoint()
224	                connection.savepoint_ids.append(sid)
225	            else:
226	                connection.savepoint_ids.append(None)
227	        else:
228	            connection.set_autocommit(
229	                False, force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True
230	            )
231	            connection.in_atomic_block = True
232	
233	        if connection.in_atomic_block:
234	            connection.atomic_blocks.append(self)
235	
236	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
237	        connection = get_connection(self.using)
238	
239	        if connection.in_atomic_block:
240	            connection.atomic_blocks.pop()
241	
242	        if connection.savepoint_ids:
243	            sid = connection.savepoint_ids.pop()
244	        else:
245	            # Prematurely unset this flag to allow using commit or rollback.
246	            connection.in_atomic_block = False
247	
248	        try:
249	            if connection.closed_in_transaction:
250	                # The database will perform a rollback by itself.
251	                # Wait until we exit the outermost block.
252	                pass
253	
254	            elif exc_type is None and not connection.needs_rollback:

264	                                # minimize overhead for the database server.

29	class AuthenticationMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
30	    def process_request(self, request):
31	        if not hasattr(request, "session"):
32	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
33	                "The Django authentication middleware requires session "
34	                "middleware to be installed. Edit your MIDDLEWARE setting to "
35	                "insert "
36	                "'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware' before "
37	                "'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware'."
38	            )
39	        request.user = SimpleLazyObject(lambda: get_user(request))
40	        request.auser = partial(auser, request)

43	class LoginRequiredMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
44	    """
45	    Middleware that redirects all unauthenticated requests to a login page.
46	
47	    Views using the login_not_required decorator will not be redirected.
48	    """
49	
50	    redirect_field_name = REDIRECT_FIELD_NAME
51	
52	    def process_view(self, request, view_func, view_args, view_kwargs):
53	        if not getattr(view_func, "login_required", True):
54	            return None
55	
56	        if request.user.is_authenticated:
57	            return None
58	
59	        return self.handle_no_permission(request, view_func)
60	
61	    def get_login_url(self, view_func):
62	        login_url = getattr(view_func, "login_url", None) or settings.LOGIN_URL
63	        if not login_url:
64	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
65	                "No login URL to redirect to. Define settings.LOGIN_URL or "
66	                "provide a login_url via the 'django.contrib.auth.decorators."
67	                "login_required' decorator."
68	            )
69	        return str(login_url)
70	
71	    def get_redirect_field_name(self, view_func):
72	        return getattr(view_func, "redirect_field_name", self.redirect_field_name)
73	
74	    def handle_no_permission(self, request, view_func):
75	        path = request.build_absolute_uri()
76	        resolved_login_url = resolve_url(self.get_login_url(view_func))
77	        # If the login url is the same scheme and net location then use the
78	        # path as the "next" url.
79	        login_scheme, login_netloc = urlsplit(resolved_login_url)[:2]
80	        current_scheme, current_netloc = urlsplit(path)[:2]
81	        if (not login_scheme or login_scheme == current_scheme) and (
82	            not login_netloc or login_netloc == current_netloc
83	        ):
84	            path = request.get_full_path()
85	
86	        return redirect_to_login(
87	            path,
88	            resolved_login_url,
89	            self.get_redirect_field_name(view_func),
90	        )

93	class RemoteUserMiddleware:
94	    """
95	    Middleware for utilizing web-server-provided authentication.
96	
97	    If request.user is not authenticated, then this middleware attempts to
98	    authenticate the username from the ``REMOTE_USER`` key in ``request.META``,
99	    an environment variable commonly set by the webserver.
100	
101	    If authentication is successful, the user is automatically logged in to
102	    persist the user in the session.
103	
104	    The ``request.META`` key is configurable and defaults to ``REMOTE_USER``.
105	    Subclass this class and change the ``header`` attribute if you need to
106	    use a different key from ``request.META``, for example a HTTP request
107	    header.
108	    """
109	
110	    sync_capable = True
111	    async_capable = True
112	
113	    def __init__(self, get_response):
114	        if get_response is None:
115	            raise ValueError("get_response must be provided.")
116	        self.get_response = get_response
117	        self.is_async = iscoroutinefunction(get_response)
118	        if self.is_async:
119	            markcoroutinefunction(self)
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	    # Name of request.META key to grab username from. Note that for
123	    # request headers, normalization to all uppercase and the addition
124	    # of a "HTTP_" prefix apply.
125	    header = "REMOTE_USER"
126	    force_logout_if_no_header = True
127	
128	    def __call__(self, request):
129	        if self.is_async:
130	            return self.__acall__(request)
131	        self.process_request(request)
132	        return self.get_response(request)
133	
134	    def process_request(self, request):
135	        # AuthenticationMiddleware is required so that request.user exists.
136	        if not hasattr(request, "user"):
137	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
138	                "The Django remote user auth middleware requires the"
139	                " authentication middleware to be installed. Edit your"
140	                " MIDDLEWARE setting to insert"
141	                " 'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware'"
142	                f" before the {self.__class__.__name__} class."
143	            )
144	        try:
145	            username = self.get_username(request)
146	        except KeyError:
147	            # If specified header doesn't exist then remove any existing
148	            # authenticated remote-user, or return (leaving request.user set to
149	            # AnonymousUser by the AuthenticationMiddleware).
150	            if self.force_logout_if_no_header and request.user.is_authenticated:
151	                self._remove_invalid_user(request)
152	            return
153	        # If the user is already authenticated and that user is the user we are
154	        # getting passed in the headers, then the correct user is already
155	        # persisted in the session and we don't need to continue.
156	        if request.user.is_authenticated:
157	            if request.user.get_username() == self.clean_username(username, request):
158	                return
159	            else:
160	                # An authenticated user is associated with the request, but
161	                # it does not match the authorized user in the header.
162	                self._remove_invalid_user(request)
163	
164	        # We are seeing this user for the first time in this session, attempt
165	        # to authenticate the user.
166	        user = auth.authenticate(request, remote_user=username)
167	        if user:
168	            # User is valid. Persist the user in the session by logging the
169	            # user in.
170	            auth.login(request, user)
171	
172	    async def __acall__(self, request):
173	        await self.aprocess_request(request)
174	        return await self.get_response(request)
175	
176	    async def aprocess_request(self, request):
177	        # AuthenticationMiddleware is required so that request.auser exists.
178	        if not hasattr(request, "auser"):
179	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
180	                "The Django remote user auth middleware requires the"
181	                " authentication middleware to be installed. Edit your"
182	                " MIDDLEWARE setting to insert"
183	                " 'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware'"
184	                f" before the {self.__class__.__name__} class."
185	            )
186	        try:
187	            username = self.get_username(request)
188	        except KeyError:
189	            # If specified header doesn't exist then remove any existing
190	            # authenticated remote-user, or return (leaving request.user set to
191	            # AnonymousUser by the AuthenticationMiddleware).
192	            if self.force_logout_if_no_header:
193	                user = await request.auser()
194	                if user.is_authenticated:
195	                    await self._aremove_invalid_user(request)
196	            return
197	        user = await request.auser()
198	        # If the user is already authenticated and that user is the user we are
199	        # getting passed in the headers, then the correct user is already
200	        # persisted in the session and we don't need to continue.
201	        if user.is_authenticated:
202	            if user.get_username() == await self.aclean_username(username, request):
203	                return
204	            else:
205	                # An authenticated user is associated with the request, but
206	                # it does not match the authorized user in the header.
207	                await self._aremove_invalid_user(request)
208	
209	        # We are seeing this user for the first time in this session, attempt
210	        # to authenticate the user.
211	        user = await auth.aauthenticate(request, remote_user=username)
212	        if user:
213	            # User is valid. Persist the user in the session by logging the
214	            # user in.
215	            await auth.alogin(request, user)
216	
217	    def clean_username(self, username, request):
218	        """
219	        Allow the backend to clean the username, if the backend defines a
220	        clean_username method.
221	        """
222	        backend_str = request.session[auth.BACKEND_SESSION_KEY]
223	        backend = auth.load_backend(backend_str)
224	        try:
225	            username = backend.clean_username(username)
226	        except AttributeError:  # Backend has no clean_username method.
227	            pass
228	        return username
229	
230	    async def aclean_username(self, username, request):
231	        """See clean_username."""
232	        backend_str = await request.session.aget(auth.BACKEND_SESSION_KEY)
233	        backend = auth.load_backend(backend_str)
234	        try:
235	            username = backend.clean_username(username)
236	        except AttributeError:  # Backend has no clean_username method.
237	            pass
238	        return username

240	    def get_username(self, request):
241	        if (
242	            isinstance(request, ASGIRequest)
243	            and self.header == RemoteUserMiddleware.header
244	        ):

246	        return request.META[self.header]

248	    def _remove_invalid_user(self, request):
249	        """

252	        """

254	            stored_backend = load_backend(

256	            )
257	        except ImportError:
258	            # backend failed to load
259	            auth.logout(request)
260	        else:
261	            if isinstance(stored_backend, RemoteUserBackend):
262	                auth.logout(request)
263	
264	    async def _aremove_invalid_user(self, request):
265	        """
266	        Remove the current authenticated user in the request which is invalid
267	        but only if the user is authenticated via the RemoteUserBackend.
268	        """
269	        try:
270	            stored_backend = load_backend(
271	                await request.session.aget(auth.BACKEND_SESSION_KEY, "")
272	            )
273	        except ImportError:
274	            # Backend failed to load.
275	            await auth.alogout(request)
276	        else:
277	            if isinstance(stored_backend, RemoteUserBackend):
278	                await auth.alogout(request)

121	    def accepted_type(self, media_type):

127	            (
128	                accepted_type

135	    def get_preferred_type(self, media_types):
136	        """Select the preferred media type from the provided options."""
137	        if not media_types or not self.accepted_types:
138	            return None

140	        desired_types = [
141	            (accepted_type, media_type)
142	            for media_type in media_types
143	            if (accepted_type := self.accepted_type(media_type)) is not None
144	        ]
145	
146	        if not desired_types:

263	    def build_absolute_uri(self, location=None):

273	            # Make it an absolute url (but schemeless and domainless) for the

277	            # Coerce lazy locations.
278	            location = str(location)

280	        if not (bits.scheme and bits.netloc):
281	            # Handle the simple, most common case. If the location is absolute

283	            # urljoin() as long as no path segments are '.' or '..'.

285	                bits.path.startswith("/")

297	                # allows the provided location to apply query strings to the
298	                # base path.
299	                location = urljoin(self._current_scheme_host + self.path, location)

529	    HTTP_PREFIX = "HTTP_"

531	    UNPREFIXED_HEADERS = {"CONTENT_TYPE", "CONTENT_LENGTH"}

533	    def __init__(self, environ):

535	        for header, value in environ.items():

538	                headers[name] = value
539	        super().__init__(headers)

543	        return super().__getitem__(key.replace("_", "-"))

545	    @classmethod
546	    def parse_header_name(cls, header):
547	        if header.startswith(cls.HTTP_PREFIX):
548	            header = header.removeprefix(cls.HTTP_PREFIX)
549	        elif header not in cls.UNPREFIXED_HEADERS:
550	            return None
551	        return header.replace("_", "-").title()

553	    @classmethod
554	    def to_wsgi_name(cls, header):
555	        header = header.replace("-", "_").upper()
556	        if header in cls.UNPREFIXED_HEADERS:
557	            return header
558	        return f"{cls.HTTP_PREFIX}{header}"

561	    def to_asgi_name(cls, header):
562	        return header.replace("-", "_").upper()

564	    @classmethod

566	        return {
567	            cls.to_wsgi_name(header_name): value
568	            for header_name, value in headers.items()

579	class QueryDict(MultiValueDict):

581	    A specialized MultiValueDict which represents a query string.
582	
583	    A QueryDict can be used to represent GET or POST data. It subclasses
584	    MultiValueDict since keys in such data can be repeated, for instance
585	    in the data from a form with a <select multiple> field.
586	
587	    By default QueryDicts are immutable, though the copy() method
588	    will always return a mutable copy.

591	    encoding (DEFAULT_CHARSET by default) to str.

595	    # level so that unpickling will have valid values

597	    _encoding = None

599	    def __init__(self, query_string=None, mutable=False, encoding=None):
600	        super().__init__()
601	        self.encoding = encoding or settings.DEFAULT_CHARSET
602	        query_string = query_string or ""

604	            "keep_blank_values": True,
605	            "encoding": self.encoding,
606	            "max_num_fields": settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FIELDS,
607	        }
608	        if isinstance(query_string, bytes):
609	            # query_string normally contains URL-encoded data, a subset of
610	            # ASCII.
611	            try:
612	                query_string = query_string.decode(self.encoding)
613	            except UnicodeDecodeError:
614	                # ... but some user agents are misbehaving :-(
615	                query_string = query_string.decode("iso-8859-1")
616	        try:
617	            for key, value in parse_qsl(query_string, **parse_qsl_kwargs):
618	                self.appendlist(key, value)
619	        except ValueError as e:
620	            # ValueError can also be raised if the strict_parsing argument to
621	            # parse_qsl() is True. As that is not used by Django, assume that
622	            # the exception was raised by exceeding the value of max_num_fields

624	            raise TooManyFieldsSent(
625	                "The number of GET/POST parameters exceeded "
626	                "settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FIELDS."

630	    @classmethod
631	    def fromkeys(cls, iterable, value="", mutable=False, encoding=None):

633	        Return a new QueryDict with keys (may be repeated) from an iterable and
634	        values from value.
635	        """
636	        q = cls("", mutable=True, encoding=encoding)
637	        for key in iterable:
638	            q.appendlist(key, value)
639	        if not mutable:
640	            q._mutable = False

714	    def copy(self):

718	    def urlencode(self, safe=None):
719	        """
720	        Return an encoded string of all query string arguments.
721	
722	        `safe` specifies characters which don't require quoting, for example::
723	
724	            >>> q = QueryDict(mutable=True)
725	            >>> q['next'] = '/a&b/'
726	            >>> q.urlencode()
727	            'next=%2Fa%26b%2F'
728	            >>> q.urlencode(safe='/')
729	            'next=/a%26b/'
730	        """
731	        output = []
732	        if safe:
733	            safe = safe.encode(self.encoding)
734	
735	            def encode(k, v):
736	                return "%s=%s" % ((quote(k, safe), quote(v, safe)))
737	
738	        else:
739	
740	            def encode(k, v):
741	                return urlencode({k: v})
742	
743	        for k, list_ in self.lists():
744	            output.extend(
745	                encode(k.encode(self.encoding), str(v).encode(self.encoding))
746	                for v in list_
747	            )
748	        return "&".join(output)

752	    def __init__(self, media_type_raw_line):
753	        full_type, self.params = parse_header_parameters(
754	            media_type_raw_line if media_type_raw_line else ""
755	        )
756	        self.main_type, _, self.sub_type = full_type.partition("/")

758	    def __str__(self):
759	        params_str = "".join("; %s=%s" % (k, v) for k, v in self.params.items())
760	        return "%s%s%s" % (
761	            self.main_type,
762	            ("/%s" % self.sub_type) if self.sub_type else "",
763	            params_str,
764	        )

766	    def __repr__(self):
767	        return "<%s: %s>" % (self.__class__.__qualname__, self)

769	    @cached_property
770	    def range_params(self):
771	        params = self.params.copy()
772	        params.pop("q", None)
773	        return params
774	
775	    def match(self, other):
776	        if not other:
777	            return False
778	
779	        if not isinstance(other, MediaType):
780	            other = MediaType(other)
781	
782	        main_types = [self.main_type, other.main_type]
783	        sub_types = [self.sub_type, other.sub_type]
784	
785	        # Main types and sub types must be defined.
786	        if not all((*main_types, *sub_types)):
787	            return False
788	
789	        # Main types must match or one be "*", same for sub types.
790	        for this_type, other_type in (main_types, sub_types):
791	            if this_type != other_type and this_type != "*" and other_type != "*":
792	                return False
793	
794	        if bool(self.range_params) == bool(other.range_params):
795	            # If both have params or neither have params, they must be
796	            # identical.
797	            result = self.range_params == other.range_params
798	        else:
799	            # If self has params and other does not, it's a match.
800	            # If other has params and self does not, don't match.
801	            result = bool(self.range_params or not other.range_params)
802	        return result
803	
804	    @cached_property
805	    def quality(self):
806	        try:
807	            quality = float(self.params.get("q", 1))
808	        except ValueError:
809	            # Discard invalid values.
810	            return 1
811	
812	        # Valid quality values must be between 0 and 1.
813	        if quality < 0 or quality > 1:
814	            return 1
815	
816	        return round(quality, 3)
817	
818	    @property
819	    def specificity(self):
820	        """
821	        Return a value from 0-3 for how specific the media type is.
822	        """
823	        if self.main_type == "*":
824	            return 0
825	        elif self.sub_type == "*":
826	            return 1
827	        elif not self.range_params:
828	            return 2
829	        return 3

832	# It's neither necessary nor appropriate to use
833	# django.utils.encoding.force_str() for parsing URLs and form inputs. Thus,
834	# this slightly more restricted function, used by QueryDict.
835	def bytes_to_text(s, encoding):
836	    """
837	    Convert bytes objects to strings, using the given encoding. Illegally
838	    encoded input characters are replaced with Unicode "unknown" codepoint
839	    (\ufffd).
840	
841	    Return any non-bytes objects without change.
842	    """
843	    if isinstance(s, bytes):
844	        return str(s, encoding, "replace")
845	    else:
846	        return s

849	def split_domain_port(host):
850	    """
851	    Return a (domain, port) tuple from a given host.
852	
853	    Returned domain is lowercased. If the host is invalid, the domain will be
854	    empty.
855	    """
856	    if match := host_validation_re.fullmatch(host.lower()):
857	        domain, port = match.groups(default="")
858	        # Remove a trailing dot (if present) from the domain.
859	        return domain.removesuffix("."), port
860	    return "", ""

863	def validate_host(host, allowed_hosts):
864	    """
865	    Validate the given host for this site.
866	
867	    Check that the host looks valid and matches a host or host pattern in the
868	    given list of ``allowed_hosts``. Any pattern beginning with a period
869	    matches a domain and all its subdomains (e.g. ``.example.com`` matches
870	    ``example.com`` and any subdomain), ``*`` matches anything, and anything
871	    else must match exactly.
872	
873	    Note: This function assumes that the given host is lowercased and has
874	    already had the port, if any, stripped off.
875	
876	    Return ``True`` for a valid host, ``False`` otherwise.
877	    """
878	    return any(
879	        pattern == "*" or is_same_domain(host, pattern) for pattern in allowed_hosts
880	    )

6	class MessageMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
7	    """
8	    Middleware that handles temporary messages.
9	    """
10	
11	    def process_request(self, request):
12	        request._messages = default_storage(request)
13	
14	    def process_response(self, request, response):
15	        """
16	        Update the storage backend (i.e., save the messages).
17	
18	        Raise ValueError if not all messages could be stored and DEBUG is True.
19	        """
20	        # A higher middleware layer may return a request which does not contain
21	        # messages storage, so make no assumption that it will be there.
22	        if hasattr(request, "_messages"):
23	            unstored_messages = request._messages.update(response)
24	            if unstored_messages and settings.DEBUG:
25	                raise ValueError("Not all temporary messages could be stored.")
26	        return response

14	class ContextDict(dict):
15	    def __init__(self, context, *args, **kwargs):
16	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
17	
18	        context.dicts.append(self)

28	class BaseContext:
29	    def __init__(self, dict_=None):
30	        self._reset_dicts(dict_)
31	
32	    def _reset_dicts(self, value=None):
33	        builtins = {"True": True, "False": False, "None": None}
34	        self.dicts = [builtins]
35	        if isinstance(value, BaseContext):
36	            self.dicts += value.dicts[1:]
37	        elif value is not None:
38	            self.dicts.append(value)
39	
40	    def __copy__(self):
41	        duplicate = BaseContext()
42	        duplicate.__class__ = self.__class__
43	        duplicate.__dict__ = copy(self.__dict__)
44	        duplicate.dicts = self.dicts[:]
45	        return duplicate
46	
47	    def __repr__(self):
48	        return repr(self.dicts)
49	
50	    def __iter__(self):
51	        return reversed(self.dicts)
52	
53	    def push(self, *args, **kwargs):
54	        dicts = []
55	        for d in args:
56	            if isinstance(d, BaseContext):
57	                dicts += d.dicts[1:]
58	            else:
59	                dicts.append(d)
60	        return ContextDict(self, *dicts, **kwargs)
61	
62	    def pop(self):
63	        if len(self.dicts) == 1:
64	            raise ContextPopException
65	        return self.dicts.pop()
66	
67	    def __setitem__(self, key, value):
68	        "Set a variable in the current context"
69	        self.dicts[-1][key] = value
70	
71	    def set_upward(self, key, value):
72	        """
73	        Set a variable in one of the higher contexts if it exists there,
74	        otherwise in the current context.
75	        """
76	        context = self.dicts[-1]
77	        for d in reversed(self.dicts):
78	            if key in d:
79	                context = d
80	                break
81	        context[key] = value
82	
83	    def __getitem__(self, key):
84	        """
85	        Get a variable's value, starting at the current context and going
86	        upward
87	        """
88	        for d in reversed(self.dicts):
89	            if key in d:
90	                return d[key]
91	        raise KeyError(key)
92	
93	    def __delitem__(self, key):
94	        "Delete a variable from the current context"
95	        del self.dicts[-1][key]
96	
97	    def __contains__(self, key):
98	        return any(key in d for d in self.dicts)
99	
100	    def get(self, key, otherwise=None):
101	        for d in reversed(self.dicts):
102	            if key in d:
103	                return d[key]
104	        return otherwise
105	
106	    def setdefault(self, key, default=None):
107	        try:
108	            return self[key]
109	        except KeyError:
110	            self[key] = default
111	        return default
112	
113	    def new(self, values=None):
114	        """
115	        Return a new context with the same properties, but with only the
116	        values given in 'values' stored.
117	        """
118	        new_context = copy(self)
119	        new_context._reset_dicts(values)
120	        return new_context
121	
122	    def flatten(self):
123	        """
124	        Return self.dicts as one dictionary.
125	        """
126	        flat = {}
127	        for d in self.dicts:
128	            flat.update(d)
129	        return flat
130	
131	    def __eq__(self, other):
132	        """
133	        Compare two contexts by comparing theirs 'dicts' attributes.
134	        """
135	        if not isinstance(other, BaseContext):
136	            return NotImplemented
137	        # flatten dictionaries because they can be put in a different order.
138	        return self.flatten() == other.flatten()

141	class Context(BaseContext):
142	    "A stack container for variable context"
143	
144	    def __init__(self, dict_=None, autoescape=True, use_l10n=None, use_tz=None):
145	        self.autoescape = autoescape
146	        self.use_l10n = use_l10n
147	        self.use_tz = use_tz
148	        self.template_name = "unknown"
149	        self.render_context = RenderContext()
150	        # Set to the original template -- as opposed to extended or included
151	        # templates -- during rendering, see bind_template.
152	        self.template = None
153	        super().__init__(dict_)
154	
155	    @contextmanager
156	    def bind_template(self, template):
157	        if self.template is not None:
158	            raise RuntimeError("Context is already bound to a template")
159	        self.template = template
160	        try:
161	            yield
162	        finally:
163	            self.template = None
164	
165	    def __copy__(self):
166	        duplicate = super().__copy__()
167	        duplicate.render_context = copy(self.render_context)
168	        return duplicate
169	
170	    def update(self, other_dict):
171	        "Push other_dict to the stack of dictionaries in the Context"
172	        if not hasattr(other_dict, "__getitem__"):
173	            raise TypeError("other_dict must be a mapping (dictionary-like) object.")
174	        if isinstance(other_dict, BaseContext):
175	            other_dict = other_dict.dicts[1:].pop()
176	        return ContextDict(self, other_dict)

179	class RenderContext(BaseContext):
180	    """
181	    A stack container for storing Template state.
182	
183	    RenderContext simplifies the implementation of template Nodes by providing
184	    a safe place to store state between invocations of a node's `render`
185	    method.
186	
187	    The RenderContext also provides scoping rules that are more sensible for
188	    'template local' variables. The render context stack is pushed before each
189	    template is rendered, creating a fresh scope with nothing in it. Name
190	    resolution fails if a variable is not found at the top of the
191	    RequestContext stack. Thus, variables are local to a specific template and
192	    don't affect the rendering of other templates as they would if they were
193	    stored in the normal template context.
194	    """
195	
196	    template = None
197	
198	    def __iter__(self):
199	        yield from self.dicts[-1]
200	
201	    def __contains__(self, key):
202	        return key in self.dicts[-1]
203	
204	    def get(self, key, otherwise=None):
205	        return self.dicts[-1].get(key, otherwise)
206	
207	    def __getitem__(self, key):
208	        return self.dicts[-1][key]
209	
210	    @contextmanager
211	    def push_state(self, template, isolated_context=True):
212	        initial = self.template
213	        self.template = template
214	        if isolated_context:
215	            self.push()
216	        try:
217	            yield
218	        finally:
219	            self.template = initial
220	            if isolated_context:
221	                self.pop()
222

224	class RequestContext(Context):
225	    """
226	    This subclass of template.Context automatically populates itself using
227	    the processors defined in the engine's configuration.
228	    Additional processors can be specified as a list of callables
229	    using the "processors" keyword argument.
230	    """
231	
232	    def __init__(
233	        self,
234	        request,
235	        dict_=None,
236	        processors=None,
237	        use_l10n=None,
238	        use_tz=None,
239	        autoescape=True,
240	    ):
241	        super().__init__(dict_, use_l10n=use_l10n, use_tz=use_tz, autoescape=autoescape)
242	        self.request = request
243	        self._processors = () if processors is None else tuple(processors)
244	        self._processors_index = len(self.dicts)
245	
246	        # placeholder for context processors output
247	        self.update({})
248	
249	        # empty dict for any new modifications
250	        # (so that context processors don't overwrite them)
251	        self.update({})
252	
253	    @contextmanager
254	    def bind_template(self, template):
255	        if self.template is not None:
256	            raise RuntimeError("Context is already bound to a template")
257	
258	        self.template = template
259	        # Set context processors according to the template engine's settings.
260	        processors = template.engine.template_context_processors + self._processors
261	        updates = {}
262	        for processor in processors:
263	            context = processor(self.request)
264	            try:
265	                updates.update(context)
266	            except TypeError as e:
267	                raise TypeError(
268	                    f"Context processor {processor.__qualname__} didn't return a "
269	                    "dictionary."
270	                ) from e
271	
272	        self.dicts[self._processors_index] = updates
273	
274	        try:
275	            yield
276	        finally:
277	            self.template = None
278	            # Unset context processors.
279	            self.dicts[self._processors_index] = {}
280	
281	    def new(self, values=None):
282	        new_context = super().new(values)
283	        # This is for backwards-compatibility: RequestContexts created via
284	        # Context.new don't include values from context processors.
285	        if hasattr(new_context, "_processors_index"):
286	            del new_context._processors_index
287	        return new_context
288	
289	
290	def make_context(context, request=None, **kwargs):
291	    """
292	    Create a suitable Context from a plain dict and optionally an HttpRequest.
293	    """
294	    if context is not None and not isinstance(context, dict):
295	        raise TypeError(
296	            "context must be a dict rather than %s." % context.__class__.__name__
297	        )
298	    if request is None:
299	        context = Context(context, **kwargs)
300	    else:
301	        # The following pattern is required to ensure values from
302	        # context override those from template context processors.
303	        original_context = context
304	        context = RequestContext(request, **kwargs)
305	        if original_context:
306	            context.push(original_context)
307	    return context

2160	            self.model._meta.db_returning_fields
2161	            if (

2163	                and (on_conflict is None or on_conflict == OnConflict.UPDATE)
2164	            )
2165	            else None
2166	        )
2167	        batches = [objs[i : i + batch_size] for i in range(0, len(objs), batch_size)]
2168	        if len(batches) > 1:
2169	            context = transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False)
2170	        else:
2171	            context = nullcontext()

2175	                    self._insert(
2176	                        item,
2177	                        fields=fields,
2178	                        using=self.db,
2179	                        on_conflict=on_conflict,
2180	                        update_fields=update_fields,
2181	                        unique_fields=unique_fields,
2182	                        returning_fields=returning_fields,

2187	    def _disable_cloning(self):

2190	        All subsequent QuerySet mutations will occur on this instance until

2196	    def _enable_cloning(self):

2200	        QuerySet instance. Necessary only when there has been a
2201	        _disable_cloning() call previously.
2202	        """

2206	    def _avoid_cloning(self):

2210	        all operations (e.g. filter(), exclude(), etc.) will mutate the same
2211	        QuerySet instance.

2214	        reasons.

2218	    def _chain(self):

2221	        operation.

2224	        _disable_cloning() temporarily, the copy doesn't occur and instead the
2225	        same QuerySet instance will be modified.

2228	            obj = self
2229	        else:
2230	            obj = self._clone()

2232	            obj.query.filter_is_sticky = True

2236	    def _clone(self):

2239	        to deepcopy().
2240	        """
2241	        c = self.__class__(
2242	            model=self.model,
2243	            query=self.query.chain(),
2244	            using=self._db,
2245	            hints=self._hints,
2246	        )
2247	        c._sticky_filter = self._sticky_filter
2248	        c._for_write = self._for_write
2249	        c._prefetch_related_lookups = self._prefetch_related_lookups[:]

2256	    def _fetch_all(self):

2259	        if self._prefetch_related_lookups and not self._prefetch_done:
2260	            self._prefetch_related_objects()

2262	    def _next_is_sticky(self):
2263	        """
2264	        Indicate that the next filter call and the one following that should
2265	        be treated as a single filter. This is only important when it comes to
2266	        determining when to reuse tables for many-to-many filters. Required so
2267	        that we can filter naturally on the results of related managers.

2270	        "self"). The method is only used internally and should be immediately
2271	        followed by a filter() that does create a clone.

2274	        return self

2276	    def _merge_sanity_check(self, other):
2277	        """Check that two QuerySet classes may be merged."""
2278	        if self._fields is not None and (
2279	            set(self.query.values_select) != set(other.query.values_select)
2280	            or set(self.query.extra_select) != set(other.query.extra_select)
2281	            or set(self.query.annotation_select) != set(other.query.annotation_select)
2282	        ):
2283	            raise TypeError(
2284	                "Merging '%s' classes must involve the same values in each case."
2285	                % self.__class__.__name__
2286	            )
2287	
2288	    def _merge_known_related_objects(self, other):
2289	        """
2290	        Keep track of all known related objects from either QuerySet instance.
2291	        """
2292	        for field, objects in other._known_related_objects.items():
2293	            self._known_related_objects.setdefault(field, {}).update(objects)
2294	
2295	    def resolve_expression(self, *args, **kwargs):
2296	        query = self.query.resolve_expression(*args, **kwargs)
2297	        query._db = self._db
2298	        return query

300	                            # If rolling back to a savepoint fails, mark for
301	                            # rollback at a higher level and avoid shadowing
302	                            # the original exception.
303	                            connection.needs_rollback = True
304	                else:
305	                    # Roll back transaction
306	                    try:
307	                        connection.rollback()
308	                    except Error:
309	                        # An error during rollback means that something
310	                        # went wrong with the connection. Drop it.
311	                        connection.close()
312	
313	        finally:
314	            # Outermost block exit when autocommit was enabled.
315	            if not connection.in_atomic_block:
316	                if connection.closed_in_transaction:
317	                    connection.connection = None
318	                else:
319	                    connection.set_autocommit(True)
320	            # Outermost block exit when autocommit was disabled.
321	            elif not connection.savepoint_ids and not connection.commit_on_exit:
322	                if connection.closed_in_transaction:
323	                    connection.connection = None
324	                else:
325	                    connection.in_atomic_block = False
326	
327	
328	def atomic(using=None, savepoint=True, durable=False):
329	    # Bare decorator: @atomic -- although the first argument is called
330	    # `using`, it's actually the function being decorated.
331	    if callable(using):
332	        return Atomic(DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS, savepoint, durable)(using)
333	    # Decorator: @atomic(...) or context manager: with atomic(...): ...
334	    else:
335	        return Atomic(using, savepoint, durable)
336	
337	
338	def _non_atomic_requests(view, using):
339	    try:
340	        view._non_atomic_requests.add(using)
341	    except AttributeError:
342	        view._non_atomic_requests = {using}
343	    return view
344	
345	
346	def non_atomic_requests(using=None):
347	    if callable(using):
348	        return _non_atomic_requests(using, DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS)
349	    else:
350	        if using is None:
351	            using = DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS
352	        return lambda view: _non_atomic_requests(view, using)
353

1	"""
2	Clickjacking Protection Middleware.
3	
4	This module provides a middleware that implements protection against a
5	malicious site loading resources from your site in a hidden frame.
6	"""
7	
8	from django.conf import settings
9	from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin
10	
11	
12	class XFrameOptionsMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
13	    """
14	    Set the X-Frame-Options HTTP header in HTTP responses.
15	
16	    Do not set the header if it's already set or if the response contains
17	    a xframe_options_exempt value set to True.
18	
19	    By default, set the X-Frame-Options header to 'DENY', meaning the response
20	    cannot be displayed in a frame, regardless of the site attempting to do so.
21	    To enable the response to be loaded on a frame within the same site, set
22	    X_FRAME_OPTIONS in your project's Django settings to 'SAMEORIGIN'.
23	    """
24	
25	    def process_response(self, request, response):
26	        # Don't set it if it's already in the response
27	        if response.get("X-Frame-Options") is not None:
28	            return response
29	
30	        # Don't set it if they used @xframe_options_exempt
31	        if getattr(response, "xframe_options_exempt", False):
32	            return response
33	
34	        response.headers["X-Frame-Options"] = self.get_xframe_options_value(
35	            request,
36	            response,
37	        )
38	        return response
39	
40	    def get_xframe_options_value(self, request, response):
41	        """
42	        Get the value to set for the X_FRAME_OPTIONS header. Use the value from
43	        the X_FRAME_OPTIONS setting, or 'DENY' if not set.
44	
45	        This method can be overridden if needed, allowing it to vary based on
46	        the request or response.
47	        """
48	        return getattr(settings, "X_FRAME_OPTIONS", "DENY").upper()
49

1	"""
2	Wrapper for loading templates from the filesystem.
3	"""
4	
5	from django.core.exceptions import SuspiciousFileOperation
6	from django.template import Origin, TemplateDoesNotExist
7	from django.utils._os import safe_join
8	
9	from .base import Loader as BaseLoader
10	
11	
12	class Loader(BaseLoader):
13	    def __init__(self, engine, dirs=None):
14	        super().__init__(engine)
15	        self.dirs = dirs
16	
17	    def get_dirs(self):
18	        return self.dirs if self.dirs is not None else self.engine.dirs
19	
20	    def get_contents(self, origin):
21	        try:
22	            with open(origin.name, encoding=self.engine.file_charset) as fp:
23	                return fp.read()
24	        except FileNotFoundError:
25	            raise TemplateDoesNotExist(origin)
26	
27	    def get_template_sources(self, template_name):
28	        """
29	        Return an Origin object pointing to an absolute path in each directory
30	        in template_dirs. For security reasons, if a path doesn't lie inside
31	        one of the template_dirs it is excluded from the result set.
32	        """
33	        for template_dir in self.get_dirs():
34	            try:
35	                name = safe_join(template_dir, template_name)
36	            except SuspiciousFileOperation:
37	                # The joined path was located outside of this template_dir
38	                # (it might be inside another one, so this isn't fatal).
39	                continue
40	
41	            yield Origin(
42	                name=name,
43	                template_name=template_name,
44	                loader=self,
45	            )
46

40	class ResponseHeaders(CaseInsensitiveMapping):
41	    def __init__(self, data):
42	        """
43	        Populate the initial data using __setitem__ to ensure values are
44	        correctly encoded.
45	        """
46	        self._store = {}
47	        if data:
48	            for header, value in self._unpack_items(data):
49	                self[header] = value
50	
51	    def _convert_to_charset(self, value, charset, mime_encode=False):
52	        """
53	        Convert headers key/value to ascii/latin-1 native strings.
54	        `charset` must be 'ascii' or 'latin-1'. If `mime_encode` is True and
55	        `value` can't be represented in the given charset, apply MIME-encoding.
56	        """
57	        try:
58	            if isinstance(value, str):
59	                # Ensure string is valid in given charset
60	                value.encode(charset)
61	            elif isinstance(value, bytes):
62	                # Convert bytestring using given charset
63	                value = value.decode(charset)
64	            else:
65	                value = str(value)
66	                # Ensure string is valid in given charset.
67	                value.encode(charset)
68	            if "\n" in value or "\r" in value:
69	                raise BadHeaderError(
70	                    f"Header values can't contain newlines (got {value!r})"
71	                )
72	        except UnicodeError as e:
73	            # Encoding to a string of the specified charset failed, but we
74	            # don't know what type that value was, or if it contains newlines,
75	            # which we may need to check for before sending it to be
76	            # encoded for multiple character sets.
77	            if (isinstance(value, bytes) and (b"\n" in value or b"\r" in value)) or (
78	                isinstance(value, str) and ("\n" in value or "\r" in value)
79	            ):
80	                raise BadHeaderError(
81	                    f"Header values can't contain newlines (got {value!r})"
82	                ) from e
83	            if mime_encode:
84	                value = Header(value, "utf-8", maxlinelen=sys.maxsize).encode()
85	            else:
86	                e.reason += ", HTTP response headers must be in %s format" % charset
87	                raise
88	        return value
89	
90	    def __delitem__(self, key):
91	        self.pop(key)
92	
93	    def __setitem__(self, key, value):
94	        key = self._convert_to_charset(key, "ascii")
95	        value = self._convert_to_charset(value, "latin-1", mime_encode=True)
96	        self._store[key.lower()] = (key, value)
97	
98	    def pop(self, key, default=None):
99	        return self._store.pop(key.lower(), default)
100	
101	    def setdefault(self, key, value):
102	        if key not in self:
103	            self[key] = value

126	            if content_type is None:

14	class Loader(BaseLoader):
15	    def __init__(self, engine, loaders):
16	        self.get_template_cache = {}
17	        self.loaders = engine.get_template_loaders(loaders)
18	        super().__init__(engine)
19	
20	    def get_dirs(self):
21	        for loader in self.loaders:
22	            if hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
23	                yield from loader.get_dirs()
24	
25	    def get_contents(self, origin):
26	        return origin.loader.get_contents(origin)
27	
28	    def get_template(self, template_name, skip=None):
29	        """
30	        Perform the caching that gives this loader its name. Often many of the
31	        templates attempted will be missing, so memory use is of concern here.
32	        To keep it in check, caching behavior is a little complicated when a
33	        template is not found. See ticket #26306 for more details.
34	
35	        With template debugging disabled, cache the TemplateDoesNotExist class
36	        for every missing template and raise a new instance of it after
37	        fetching it from the cache.
38	
39	        With template debugging enabled, a unique TemplateDoesNotExist object
40	        is cached for each missing template to preserve debug data. When
41	        raising an exception, Python sets __traceback__, __context__, and
42	        __cause__ attributes on it. Those attributes can contain references to
43	        all sorts of objects up the call chain and caching them creates a
44	        memory leak. Thus, unraised copies of the exceptions are cached and
45	        copies of those copies are raised after they're fetched from the cache.
46	        """
47	        key = self.cache_key(template_name, skip)
48	        cached = self.get_template_cache.get(key)
49	        if cached:
50	            if isinstance(cached, type) and issubclass(cached, TemplateDoesNotExist):
51	                raise cached(template_name)
52	            elif isinstance(cached, TemplateDoesNotExist):
53	                raise copy_exception(cached)
54	            return cached
55	
56	        try:
57	            template = super().get_template(template_name, skip)
58	        except TemplateDoesNotExist as e:
59	            self.get_template_cache[key] = (
60	                copy_exception(e) if self.engine.debug else TemplateDoesNotExist
61	            )
62	            raise
63	        else:
64	            self.get_template_cache[key] = template
65	
66	        return template
67	
68	    def get_template_sources(self, template_name):
69	        for loader in self.loaders:
70	            yield from loader.get_template_sources(template_name)
71	
72	    def cache_key(self, template_name, skip=None):
73	        """
74	        Generate a cache key for the template name and skip.
75	
76	        If skip is provided, only origins that match template_name are included
77	        in the cache key. This ensures each template is only parsed and cached
78	        once if contained in different extend chains like:
79	
80	            x -> a -> a
81	            y -> a -> a
82	            z -> a -> a
83	        """
84	        skip_prefix = ""
85	
86	        if skip:
87	            matching = [
88	                origin.name for origin in skip if origin.template_name == template_name
89	            ]
90	            if matching:
91	                skip_prefix = self.generate_hash(matching)
92	
93	        return "-".join(s for s in (str(template_name), skip_prefix) if s)
94	
95	    def generate_hash(self, values):
96	        return hashlib.sha1("|".join(values).encode()).hexdigest()
97	
98	    def reset(self):
99	        "Empty the template cache."
100	        self.get_template_cache.clear()

23	    update_wrapper(_wrapper, dummy)

26	def _multi_decorate(decorators, method):
27	    """
28	    Decorate `method` with one or more function decorators. `decorators` can be
29	    a single decorator or an iterable of decorators.
30	    """
31	    if hasattr(decorators, "__iter__"):
32	        # Apply a list/tuple of decorators if 'decorators' is one. Decorator
33	        # functions are applied so that the call order is the same as the
34	        # order in which they appear in the iterable.
35	        decorators = decorators[::-1]
36	    else:
37	        decorators = [decorators]
38	
39	    def _wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
40	        # bound_method has the signature that 'decorator' expects i.e. no
41	        # 'self' argument, but it's a closure over self so it can call
42	        # 'func'. Also, wrap method.__get__() in a function because new
43	        # attributes can't be set on bound method objects, only on functions.
44	        bound_method = wraps(method)(partial(method.__get__(self, type(self))))
45	        for dec in decorators:
46	            bound_method = dec(bound_method)
47	        return bound_method(*args, **kwargs)
48	
49	    # Copy any attributes that a decorator adds to the function it decorates.
50	    for dec in decorators:
51	        _update_method_wrapper(_wrapper, dec)
52	    # Preserve any existing attributes of 'method', including the name.
53	    update_wrapper(_wrapper, method)
54	
55	    if iscoroutinefunction(method):
56	        markcoroutinefunction(_wrapper)
57	
58	    return _wrapper

61	def method_decorator(decorator, name=""):
62	    """
63	    Convert a function decorator into a method decorator
64	    """
65	
66	    # 'obj' can be a class or a function. If 'obj' is a function at the time it
67	    # is passed to _dec,  it will eventually be a method of the class it is
68	    # defined on. If 'obj' is a class, the 'name' is required to be the name
69	    # of the method that will be decorated.
70	    def _dec(obj):
71	        if not isinstance(obj, type):
72	            return _multi_decorate(decorator, obj)
73	        if not (name and hasattr(obj, name)):
74	            raise ValueError(
75	                "The keyword argument `name` must be the name of a method "
76	                "of the decorated class: %s. Got '%s' instead." % (obj, name)
77	            )
78	        method = getattr(obj, name)
79	        if not callable(method):
80	            raise TypeError(
81	                "Cannot decorate '%s' as it isn't a callable attribute of "
82	                "%s (%s)." % (name, obj, method)
83	            )
84	        _wrapper = _multi_decorate(decorator, method)
85	        setattr(obj, name, _wrapper)
86	        return obj
87	
88	    # Don't worry about making _dec look similar to a list/tuple as it's rather
89	    # meaningless.
90	    if not hasattr(decorator, "__iter__"):
91	        update_wrapper(_dec, decorator)
92	    # Change the name to aid debugging.
93	    obj = decorator if hasattr(decorator, "__name__") else decorator.__class__
94	    _dec.__name__ = "method_decorator(%s)" % obj.__name__
95	    return _dec

98	def decorator_from_middleware_with_args(middleware_class):
99	    """
100	    Like decorator_from_middleware, but return a function
101	    that accepts the arguments to be passed to the middleware_class.
102	    Use like::
103	
104	         cache_page = decorator_from_middleware_with_args(CacheMiddleware)
105	         # ...
106	
107	         @cache_page(3600)
108	         def my_view(request):
109	             # ...
110	    """
111	    return make_middleware_decorator(middleware_class)

114	def decorator_from_middleware(middleware_class):
115	    """
116	    Given a middleware class (not an instance), return a view decorator. This
117	    lets you use middleware functionality on a per-view basis. The middleware
118	    is created with no params passed.
119	    """
120	    return make_middleware_decorator(middleware_class)()
121	
122	
123	def make_middleware_decorator(middleware_class):
124	    def _make_decorator(*m_args, **m_kwargs):
125	        def _decorator(view_func):
126	            middleware = middleware_class(view_func, *m_args, **m_kwargs)
127	
128	            def _pre_process_request(request, *args, **kwargs):
129	                if hasattr(middleware, "process_request"):
130	                    result = middleware.process_request(request)
131	                    if result is not None:
132	                        return result
133	                if hasattr(middleware, "process_view"):
134	                    result = middleware.process_view(request, view_func, args, kwargs)
135	                    if result is not None:
136	                        return result
137	                return None
138	
139	            def _process_exception(request, exception):
140	                if hasattr(middleware, "process_exception"):
141	                    result = middleware.process_exception(request, exception)
142	                    if result is not None:
143	                        return result
144	                raise
145	
146	            def _post_process_request(request, response):
147	                if hasattr(response, "render") and callable(response.render):
148	                    if hasattr(middleware, "process_template_response"):
149	                        response = middleware.process_template_response(
150	                            request, response
151	                        )
152	                    # Defer running of process_response until after the
153	                    # template has been rendered:
154	                    if hasattr(middleware, "process_response"):
155	
156	                        def callback(response):
157	                            return middleware.process_response(request, response)
158	
159	                        response.add_post_render_callback(callback)
160	                else:
161	                    if hasattr(middleware, "process_response"):
162	                        return middleware.process_response(request, response)
163	                return response
164	
165	            if iscoroutinefunction(view_func):
166	
167	                async def _view_wrapper(request, *args, **kwargs):
168	                    result = _pre_process_request(request, *args, **kwargs)
169	                    if result is not None:
170	                        return result
171	
172	                    try:
173	                        response = await view_func(request, *args, **kwargs)
174	                    except Exception as e:
175	                        result = _process_exception(request, e)
176	                        if result is not None:
177	                            return result
178	
179	                    return _post_process_request(request, response)
180	
181	            else:
182	
183	                def _view_wrapper(request, *args, **kwargs):
184	                    result = _pre_process_request(request, *args, **kwargs)
185	                    if result is not None:
186	                        return result
187	
188	                    try:
189	                        response = view_func(request, *args, **kwargs)
190	                    except Exception as e:
191	                        result = _process_exception(request, e)
192	                        if result is not None:
193	                            return result
194	
195	                    return _post_process_request(request, response)
196	
197	            return wraps(view_func)(_view_wrapper)
198	
199	        return _decorator

15	class LimitedStream(IOBase):
16	    """
17	    Wrap another stream to disallow reading it past a number of bytes.
18	
19	    Based on the implementation from werkzeug.wsgi.LimitedStream. See:
20	    https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/blob/dbf78f67/src/werkzeug/wsgi.py#L828
21	    """
22	
23	    def __init__(self, stream, limit):
24	        self._read = stream.read
25	        self._readline = stream.readline
26	        self._pos = 0
27	        self.limit = limit
28	
29	    def read(self, size=-1, /):
30	        _pos = self._pos
31	        limit = self.limit
32	        if _pos >= limit:
33	            return b""
34	        if size == -1 or size is None:
35	            size = limit - _pos
36	        else:
37	            size = min(size, limit - _pos)
38	        data = self._read(size)
39	        self._pos += len(data)
40	        return data
41	
42	    def readline(self, size=-1, /):
43	        _pos = self._pos
44	        limit = self.limit
45	        if _pos >= limit:
46	            return b""
47	        if size == -1 or size is None:
48	            size = limit - _pos
49	        else:
50	            size = min(size, limit - _pos)
51	        line = self._readline(size)
52	        self._pos += len(line)
53	        return line

56	class WSGIRequest(HttpRequest):
57	    def __init__(self, environ):
58	        script_name = get_script_name(environ)
59	        # If PATH_INFO is empty (e.g. accessing the SCRIPT_NAME URL without a
60	        # trailing slash), operate as if '/' was requested.
61	        path_info = get_path_info(environ) or "/"
62	        self.environ = environ
63	        self.path_info = path_info
64	        # be careful to only replace the first slash in the path because of
65	        # http://test/something and http://test//something being different as
66	        # stated in RFC 3986.
67	        self.path = "%s/%s" % (script_name.rstrip("/"), path_info.replace("/", "", 1))
68	        self.META = environ
69	        self.META["PATH_INFO"] = path_info
70	        self.META["SCRIPT_NAME"] = script_name
71	        self.method = environ["REQUEST_METHOD"].upper()
72	        # Set content_type, content_params, and encoding.
73	        self._set_content_type_params(environ)
74	        try:
75	            content_length = int(environ.get("CONTENT_LENGTH"))
76	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
77	            content_length = 0
78	        self._stream = LimitedStream(self.environ["wsgi.input"], content_length)
79	        self._read_started = False
80	        self.resolver_match = None
81	
82	    def _get_scheme(self):
83	        return self.environ.get("wsgi.url_scheme")
84	
85	    @cached_property
86	    def GET(self):
87	        # The WSGI spec says 'QUERY_STRING' may be absent.
88	        raw_query_string = get_bytes_from_wsgi(self.environ, "QUERY_STRING", "")
89	        return QueryDict(raw_query_string, encoding=self._encoding)
90	
91	    def _get_post(self):
92	        if not hasattr(self, "_post"):
93	            self._load_post_and_files()
94	        return self._post
95	
96	    def _set_post(self, post):
97	        self._post = post
98	
99	    @cached_property
100	    def COOKIES(self):
101	        raw_cookie = get_str_from_wsgi(self.environ, "HTTP_COOKIE", "")
102	        return parse_cookie(raw_cookie)
103	
104	    @property
105	    def FILES(self):
106	        if not hasattr(self, "_files"):
107	            self._load_post_and_files()
108	        return self._files
109	
110	    POST = property(_get_post, _set_post)

113	class WSGIHandler(base.BaseHandler):
114	    request_class = WSGIRequest
115	
116	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
117	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
118	        self.load_middleware()
119	
120	    def __call__(self, environ, start_response):
121	        set_script_prefix(get_script_name(environ))
122	        signals.request_started.send(sender=self.__class__, environ=environ)
123	        request = self.request_class(environ)
124	        response = self.get_response(request)
125	
126	        response._handler_class = self.__class__
127	
128	        status = "%d %s" % (response.status_code, response.reason_phrase)
129	        response_headers = [
130	            *response.items(),
131	            *(("Set-Cookie", c.OutputString()) for c in response.cookies.values()),
132	        ]
133	        start_response(status, response_headers)
134	        if getattr(response, "file_to_stream", None) is not None and environ.get(
135	            "wsgi.file_wrapper"
136	        ):
137	            # If `wsgi.file_wrapper` is used the WSGI server does not call
138	            # .close on the response, but on the file wrapper. Patch it to use
139	            # response.close instead which takes care of closing all files.
140	            response.file_to_stream.close = response.close
141	            response = environ["wsgi.file_wrapper"](
142	                response.file_to_stream, response.block_size
143	            )
144	        return response
145

147	def get_path_info(environ):
148	    """Return the HTTP request's PATH_INFO as a string."""
149	    path_info = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "PATH_INFO", "/")
150	
151	    return repercent_broken_unicode(path_info).decode()

153	
154	def get_script_name(environ):
155	    """
156	    Return the equivalent of the HTTP request's SCRIPT_NAME environment
157	    variable. If Apache mod_rewrite is used, return what would have been
158	    the script name prior to any rewriting (so it's the script name as seen
159	    from the client's perspective), unless the FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME setting is
160	    set (to anything).
161	    """
162	    if settings.FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME is not None:
163	        return settings.FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME
164	
165	    # If Apache's mod_rewrite had a whack at the URL, Apache set either
166	    # SCRIPT_URL or REDIRECT_URL to the full resource URL before applying any
167	    # rewrites. Unfortunately not every web server (lighttpd!) passes this
168	    # information through all the time, so FORCE_SCRIPT_NAME, above, is still
169	    # needed.
170	    script_url = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "SCRIPT_URL", "") or get_bytes_from_wsgi(
171	        environ, "REDIRECT_URL", ""
172	    )
173	
174	    if script_url:
175	        if b"//" in script_url:
176	            # mod_wsgi squashes multiple successive slashes in PATH_INFO,
177	            # do the same with script_url before manipulating paths (#17133).
178	            script_url = _slashes_re.sub(b"/", script_url)
179	        path_info = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "PATH_INFO", "")
180	        script_name = script_url.removesuffix(path_info)
181	    else:
182	        script_name = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, "SCRIPT_NAME", "")
183	
184	    return script_name.decode()
185	
186	
187	def get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, key, default):
188	    """
189	    Get a value from the WSGI environ dictionary as bytes.
190	
191	    key and default should be strings.
192	    """
193	    value = environ.get(key, default)
194	    # Non-ASCII values in the WSGI environ are arbitrarily decoded with
195	    # ISO-8859-1. This is wrong for Django websites where UTF-8 is the default.
196	    # Re-encode to recover the original bytestring.
197	    return value.encode("iso-8859-1")
198	
199	
200	def get_str_from_wsgi(environ, key, default):
201	    """
202	    Get a value from the WSGI environ dictionary as str.
203	
204	    key and default should be str objects.
205	    """
206	    value = get_bytes_from_wsgi(environ, key, default)
207	    return value.decode(errors="replace")

722	        # alias prefix of the rhs and update current aliases accordingly,

730	        change_map = {}

736	        # case a single row can't fulfill a condition like:

739	        # condition. In OR case a single True is enough, so single row is
740	        # enough, too.
741	        #

744	        # joins. This is something that could be fixed later on.
745	        reuse = set() if conjunction else set(self.alias_map)

763	            # distinct joins for the same connection in rhs query, then the
764	            # combined query must have two joins, too.
765	            reuse.discard(new_alias)

768	            if not rhs.alias_refcount[alias]:

770	                # will be unused in the new query, too. We have to add and
771	                # unref the alias so that join promotion has information of
772	                # the join type for the unused alias.
773	                self.unref_alias(new_alias)
774	        joinpromoter.add_votes(rhs_votes)
775	        joinpromoter.update_join_types(self)
776	
777	        # Combine subqueries aliases to ensure aliases relabelling properly
778	        # handle subqueries when combining where and select clauses.
779	        self.subq_aliases |= rhs.subq_aliases
780	
781	        # Now relabel a copy of the rhs where-clause and add it to the current
782	        # one.
783	        w = rhs.where.clone()
784	        w.relabel_aliases(change_map)
785	        self.where.add(w, connector)
786	
787	        # Selection columns and extra extensions are those provided by 'rhs'.
788	        if rhs.select:
789	            self.set_select([col.relabeled_clone(change_map) for col in rhs.select])
790	        else:
791	            self.select = ()
792	
793	        if connector == OR:
794	            # It would be nice to be able to handle this, but the queries don't
795	            # really make sense (or return consistent value sets). Not worth
796	            # the extra complexity when you can write a real query instead.
797	            if self.extra and rhs.extra:
798	                raise ValueError(
799	                    "When merging querysets using 'or', you cannot have "
800	                    "extra(select=...) on both sides."
801	                )
802	        self.extra.update(rhs.extra)
803	        extra_select_mask = set()
804	        if self.extra_select_mask is not None:
805	            extra_select_mask.update(self.extra_select_mask)
806	        if rhs.extra_select_mask is not None:
807	            extra_select_mask.update(rhs.extra_select_mask)
808	        if extra_select_mask:
809	            self.set_extra_mask(extra_select_mask)
810	        self.extra_tables += rhs.extra_tables
811	
812	        # Ordering uses the 'rhs' ordering, unless it has none, in which case
813	        # the current ordering is used.
814	        self.order_by = rhs.order_by or self.order_by
815	        self.extra_order_by = rhs.extra_order_by or self.extra_order_by

817	    def _get_defer_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
818	        if select_mask is None:
819	            select_mask = {}
820	        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
821	        # All concrete fields and related objects that are not part of the
822	        # defer mask must be included. If a relational field is encountered it
823	        # gets added to the mask for it be considered if `select_related` and
824	        # the cycle continues by recursively calling this function.
825	        for field in opts.concrete_fields + opts.related_objects:
826	            field_mask = mask.pop(field.name, None)
827	            field_att_mask = None
828	            if field_attname := getattr(field, "attname", None):
829	                field_att_mask = mask.pop(field_attname, None)
830	            if field_mask is None and field_att_mask is None:
831	                select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
832	            elif field_mask:
833	                if not field.is_relation:
834	                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
835	                # Virtual fields such as many-to-many and generic foreign keys
836	                # cannot be effectively deferred. Historically, they were
837	                # allowed to be passed to QuerySet.defer(). Ignore such field
838	                # references until a layer of validation at mask alteration
839	                # time is eventually implemented.
840	                if field.many_to_many:
841	                    continue
842	                field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
843	                related_model = field.related_model._meta.concrete_model
844	                self._get_defer_select_mask(
845	                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
846	                )
847	        # Remaining defer entries must be references to filtered relations
848	        # otherwise they are surfaced as missing field errors.
849	        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
850	            if filtered_relation := self._filtered_relations.get(field_name):
851	                relation = opts.get_field(filtered_relation.relation_name)
852	                field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault((field_name, relation), {})
853	                related_model = relation.related_model._meta.concrete_model
854	                self._get_defer_select_mask(
855	                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
856	                )
857	            else:
858	                opts.get_field(field_name)
859	        return select_mask
860	
861	    def _get_only_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
862	        if select_mask is None:
863	            select_mask = {}
864	        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
865	        # Only include fields mentioned in the mask.
866	        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
867	            field = opts.get_field(field_name)
868	            field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
869	            if field_mask:
870	                if not field.is_relation:
871	                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
872	                related_model = field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model
873	                self._get_only_select_mask(
874	                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
875	                )
876	        return select_mask
877	
878	    def get_select_mask(self):
879	        """
880	        Convert the self.deferred_loading data structure to an alternate data
881	        structure, describing the field that *will* be loaded. This is used to
882	        compute the columns to select from the database and also by the
883	        QuerySet class to work out which fields are being initialized on each
884	        model. Models that have all their fields included aren't mentioned in
885	        the result, only those that have field restrictions in place.
886	        """
887	        field_names, defer = self.deferred_loading
888	        if not field_names:
889	            return {}
890	        mask = {}
891	        for field_name in field_names:
892	            part_mask = mask
893	            for part in field_name.split(LOOKUP_SEP):
894	                part_mask = part_mask.setdefault(part, {})
895	        opts = self.get_meta()
896	        if defer:
897	            return self._get_defer_select_mask(opts, mask)
898	        return self._get_only_select_mask(opts, mask)

21	class BaseHandler:
22	    _view_middleware = None
