27	    def load_middleware(self, is_async=False):
28	        """
29	        Populate middleware lists from settings.MIDDLEWARE.
30	
31	        Must be called after the environment is fixed (see __call__ in
32	        subclasses).
33	        """
34	        self._view_middleware = []
35	        self._template_response_middleware = []
36	        self._exception_middleware = []
37	
38	        get_response = self._get_response_async if is_async else self._get_response
39	        handler = convert_exception_to_response(get_response)
40	        handler_is_async = is_async
41	        for middleware_path in reversed(settings.MIDDLEWARE):
42	            middleware = import_string(middleware_path)
43	            middleware_can_sync = getattr(middleware, "sync_capable", True)
44	            middleware_can_async = getattr(middleware, "async_capable", False)
45	            if not middleware_can_sync and not middleware_can_async:
46	                raise RuntimeError(
47	                    "Middleware %s must have at least one of "
48	                    "sync_capable/async_capable set to True." % middleware_path
49	                )
50	            elif not handler_is_async and middleware_can_sync:
51	                middleware_is_async = False
52	            else:
53	                middleware_is_async = middleware_can_async
54	            try:
55	                # Adapt handler, if needed.
56	                adapted_handler = self.adapt_method_mode(
57	                    middleware_is_async,
58	                    handler,
59	                    handler_is_async,
60	                    debug=settings.DEBUG,
61	                    name="middleware %s" % middleware_path,
62	                )
63	                mw_instance = middleware(adapted_handler)
64	            except MiddlewareNotUsed as exc:
65	                if settings.DEBUG:
66	                    if str(exc):
67	                        logger.debug("MiddlewareNotUsed(%r): %s", middleware_path, exc)
68	                    else:
69	                        logger.debug("MiddlewareNotUsed: %r", middleware_path)
70	                continue
71	            else:
72	                handler = adapted_handler
73	
74	            if mw_instance is None:
75	                raise ImproperlyConfigured(
76	                    "Middleware factory %s returned None." % middleware_path
77	                )
78	
79	            if hasattr(mw_instance, "process_view"):
80	                self._view_middleware.insert(
81	                    0,
82	                    self.adapt_method_mode(is_async, mw_instance.process_view),
83	                )
84	            if hasattr(mw_instance, "process_template_response"):
85	                self._template_response_middleware.append(
86	                    self.adapt_method_mode(
87	                        is_async, mw_instance.process_template_response
88	                    ),
89	                )
90	            if hasattr(mw_instance, "process_exception"):
91	                # The exception-handling stack is still always synchronous for
92	                # now, so adapt that way.
93	                self._exception_middleware.append(
94	                    self.adapt_method_mode(False, mw_instance.process_exception),
95	                )
96	
97	            handler = convert_exception_to_response(mw_instance)
98	            handler_is_async = middleware_is_async
99	
100	        # Adapt the top of the stack, if needed.
101	        handler = self.adapt_method_mode(is_async, handler, handler_is_async)
102	        # We only assign to this when initialization is complete as it is used
103	        # as a flag for initialization being complete.
104	        self._middleware_chain = handler
105	
106	    def adapt_method_mode(
107	        self,
108	        is_async,
109	        method,
110	        method_is_async=None,
111	        debug=False,
112	        name=None,
113	    ):
114	        """
115	        Adapt a method to be in the correct "mode":
116	        - If is_async is False:
117	          - Synchronous methods are left alone
118	          - Asynchronous methods are wrapped with async_to_sync
119	        - If is_async is True:
120	          - Synchronous methods are wrapped with sync_to_async()
121	          - Asynchronous methods are left alone
122	        """
123	        if method_is_async is None:
124	            method_is_async = iscoroutinefunction(method)
125	        if debug and not name:
126	            name = name or "method %s()" % method.__qualname__
127	        if is_async:
128	            if not method_is_async:
129	                if debug:
130	                    logger.debug("Synchronous handler adapted for %s.", name)
131	                return sync_to_async(method, thread_sensitive=True)
132	        elif method_is_async:
133	            if debug:
134	                logger.debug("Asynchronous handler adapted for %s.", name)
135	            return async_to_sync(method)
136	        return method

138	    def get_response(self, request):
139	        """Return an HttpResponse object for the given HttpRequest."""
140	        # Setup default url resolver for this thread
141	        set_urlconf(settings.ROOT_URLCONF)
142	        response = self._middleware_chain(request)
143	        response._resource_closers.append(request.close)
144	        if response.status_code >= 400:
145	            log_response(
146	                "%s: %s",
147	                response.reason_phrase,
148	                request.path,
149	                response=response,
150	                request=request,
151	            )
152	        return response

154	    async def get_response_async(self, request):
155	        """
156	        Asynchronous version of get_response.
157	
158	        Funneling everything, including WSGI, into a single async
159	        get_response() is too slow. Avoid the context switch by using
160	        a separate async response path.
161	        """
162	        # Setup default url resolver for this thread.
163	        set_urlconf(settings.ROOT_URLCONF)
164	        response = await self._middleware_chain(request)
165	        response._resource_closers.append(request.close)
166	        if response.status_code >= 400:
167	            await sync_to_async(log_response, thread_sensitive=False)(
168	                "%s: %s",
169	                response.reason_phrase,
170	                request.path,
171	                response=response,
172	                request=request,
173	            )
174	        return response

176	    def _get_response(self, request):
177	        """
178	        Resolve and call the view, then apply view, exception, and
179	        template_response middleware. This method is everything that happens
180	        inside the request/response middleware.
181	        """
182	        response = None
183	        callback, callback_args, callback_kwargs = self.resolve_request(request)
184	
185	        # Apply view middleware
186	        for middleware_method in self._view_middleware:
187	            response = middleware_method(
188	                request, callback, callback_args, callback_kwargs
189	            )
190	            if response:
191	                break
192	
193	        if response is None:
194	            wrapped_callback = self.make_view_atomic(callback)
195	            # If it is an asynchronous view, run it in a subthread.
196	            if iscoroutinefunction(wrapped_callback):
197	                wrapped_callback = async_to_sync(wrapped_callback)
198	            try:
199	                response = wrapped_callback(request, *callback_args, **callback_kwargs)
200	            except Exception as e:
201	                response = self.process_exception_by_middleware(e, request)
202	                if response is None:
203	                    raise
204	
205	        # Complain if the view returned None (a common error).
206	        self.check_response(response, callback)
207	
208	        # If the response supports deferred rendering, apply template
209	        # response middleware and then render the response
210	        if hasattr(response, "render") and callable(response.render):
211	            for middleware_method in self._template_response_middleware:
212	                response = middleware_method(request, response)
213	                # Complain if the template response middleware returned None
214	                # (a common error).
215	                self.check_response(
216	                    response,
217	                    middleware_method,
218	                    name="%s.process_template_response"
219	                    % (middleware_method.__self__.__class__.__name__,),
220	                )
221	            try:
222	                response = response.render()
223	            except Exception as e:
224	                response = self.process_exception_by_middleware(e, request)
225	                if response is None:
226	                    raise
227	
228	        return response

302	    def resolve_request(self, request):
303	        """
304	        Retrieve/set the urlconf for the request. Return the view resolved,
305	        with its args and kwargs.
306	        """
307	        # Work out the resolver.
308	        if hasattr(request, "urlconf"):
309	            urlconf = request.urlconf
310	            set_urlconf(urlconf)
311	            resolver = get_resolver(urlconf)
312	        else:
313	            resolver = get_resolver()
314	        # Resolve the view, and assign the match object back to the request.
315	        resolver_match = resolver.resolve(request.path_info)
316	        request.resolver_match = resolver_match
317	        return resolver_match

319	    def check_response(self, response, callback, name=None):
320	        """
321	        Raise an error if the view returned None or an uncalled coroutine.
322	        """
323	        if not (response is None or asyncio.iscoroutine(response)):
324	            return
325	        if not name:
326	            if isinstance(callback, types.FunctionType):  # FBV
327	                name = "The view %s.%s" % (callback.__module__, callback.__name__)
328	            else:  # CBV
329	                name = "The view %s.%s.__call__" % (
330	                    callback.__module__,
331	                    callback.__class__.__name__,
332	                )
333	        if response is None:
334	            raise ValueError(
335	                "%s didn't return an HttpResponse object. It returned None "
336	                "instead." % name
337	            )
338	        elif asyncio.iscoroutine(response):
339	            raise ValueError(
340	                "%s didn't return an HttpResponse object. It returned an "
341	                "unawaited coroutine instead. You may need to add an 'await' "
342	                "into your view." % name
343	            )

347	    def make_view_atomic(self, view):
348	        non_atomic_requests = getattr(view, "_non_atomic_requests", set())
349	        for alias, settings_dict in connections.settings.items():
350	            if settings_dict["ATOMIC_REQUESTS"] and alias not in non_atomic_requests:
351	                if iscoroutinefunction(view):
352	                    raise RuntimeError(
353	                        "You cannot use ATOMIC_REQUESTS with async views."
354	                    )
355	                view = transaction.atomic(using=alias)(view)
356	        return view

358	    def process_exception_by_middleware(self, exception, request):
359	        """
360	        Pass the exception to the exception middleware. If no middleware
361	        return a response for this exception, return None.
362	        """
363	        for middleware_method in self._exception_middleware:
364	            response = middleware_method(request, exception)
365	            if response:
366	                return response
367	        return None

6	class ContentNotRenderedError(Exception):
7	    pass
8	
9	
10	class SimpleTemplateResponse(HttpResponse):
11	    rendering_attrs = ["template_name", "context_data", "_post_render_callbacks"]
12	
13	    def __init__(
14	        self,
15	        template,
16	        context=None,
17	        content_type=None,
18	        status=None,
19	        charset=None,
20	        using=None,
21	        headers=None,
22	    ):
23	        # It would seem obvious to call these next two members 'template' and
24	        # 'context', but those names are reserved as part of the test Client
25	        # API. To avoid the name collision, we use different names.
26	        self.template_name = template
27	        self.context_data = context
28	
29	        self.using = using
30	
31	        self._post_render_callbacks = []
32	
33	        # _request stores the current request object in subclasses that know
34	        # about requests, like TemplateResponse. It's defined in the base class
35	        # to minimize code duplication.
36	        # It's called self._request because self.request gets overwritten by
37	        # django.test.client.Client. Unlike template_name and context_data,
38	        # _request should not be considered part of the public API.
39	        self._request = None
40	
41	        # content argument doesn't make sense here because it will be replaced
42	        # with rendered template so we always pass empty string in order to
43	        # prevent errors and provide shorter signature.
44	        super().__init__("", content_type, status, charset=charset, headers=headers)
45	
46	        # _is_rendered tracks whether the template and context has been baked
47	        # into a final response.
48	        # Super __init__ doesn't know any better than to set self.content to
49	        # the empty string we just gave it, which wrongly sets _is_rendered
50	        # True, so we initialize it to False after the call to super __init__.
51	        self._is_rendered = False
52	
53	    def __getstate__(self):
54	        """
55	        Raise an exception if trying to pickle an unrendered response. Pickle
56	        only rendered data, not the data used to construct the response.
57	        """
58	        obj_dict = self.__dict__.copy()
59	        if not self._is_rendered:
60	            raise ContentNotRenderedError(
61	                "The response content must be rendered before it can be pickled."
62	            )
63	        for attr in self.rendering_attrs:
64	            if attr in obj_dict:
65	                del obj_dict[attr]
66	
67	        return obj_dict
68	
69	    def resolve_template(self, template):
70	        """Accept a template object, path-to-template, or list of paths."""
71	        if isinstance(template, (list, tuple)):
72	            return select_template(template, using=self.using)
73	        elif isinstance(template, str):
74	            return get_template(template, using=self.using)
75	        else:
76	            return template
77	
78	    def resolve_context(self, context):
79	        return context
80	
81	    @property
82	    def rendered_content(self):
83	        """Return the freshly rendered content for the template and context
84	        described by the TemplateResponse.
85	
86	        This *does not* set the final content of the response. To set the
87	        response content, you must either call render(), or set the
88	        content explicitly using the value of this property.
89	        """
90	        template = self.resolve_template(self.template_name)
91	        context = self.resolve_context(self.context_data)
92	        return template.render(context, self._request)
93	
94	    def add_post_render_callback(self, callback):
95	        """Add a new post-rendering callback.
96	
97	        If the response has already been rendered,
98	        invoke the callback immediately.
99	        """
100	        if self._is_rendered:
101	            callback(self)
102	        else:
103	            self._post_render_callbacks.append(callback)
104	
105	    def render(self):
106	        """Render (thereby finalizing) the content of the response.
107	
108	        If the content has already been rendered, this is a no-op.
109	
110	        Return the baked response instance.
111	        """
112	        retval = self
113	        if not self._is_rendered:
114	            self.content = self.rendered_content
115	            for post_callback in self._post_render_callbacks:
116	                newretval = post_callback(retval)
117	                if newretval is not None:
118	                    retval = newretval
119	        return retval
120	
121	    @property
122	    def is_rendered(self):
123	        return self._is_rendered
124	
125	    def __iter__(self):
126	        if not self._is_rendered:
127	            raise ContentNotRenderedError(
128	                "The response content must be rendered before it can be iterated over."
129	            )
130	        return super().__iter__()
131	
132	    @property
133	    def content(self):
134	        if not self._is_rendered:
135	            raise ContentNotRenderedError(
136	                "The response content must be rendered before it can be accessed."
137	            )
138	        return super().content
139	
140	    @content.setter
141	    def content(self, value):
142	        """Set the content for the response."""
143	        HttpResponse.content.fset(self, value)
144	        self._is_rendered = True
145	
146	
147	class TemplateResponse(SimpleTemplateResponse):
148	    rendering_attrs = [*SimpleTemplateResponse.rendering_attrs, "_request"]
149	
150	    def __init__(
151	        self,
152	        request,
153	        template,
154	        context=None,
155	        content_type=None,
156	        status=None,
157	        charset=None,
158	        using=None,
159	        headers=None,
160	    ):
161	        super().__init__(
162	            template, context, content_type, status, charset, using, headers=headers
163	        )
164	        self._request = request

5	from django.utils.decorators import decorator_from_middleware
6	
7	csrf_protect = decorator_from_middleware(CsrfViewMiddleware)
8	csrf_protect.__name__ = "csrf_protect"
9	csrf_protect.__doc__ = """
10	This decorator adds CSRF protection in exactly the same way as
11	CsrfViewMiddleware, but it can be used on a per view basis. Using both, or
12	using the decorator multiple times, is harmless and efficient.
13	"""
14	
15	
16	class _EnsureCsrfToken(CsrfViewMiddleware):
17	    # Behave like CsrfViewMiddleware but don't reject requests or log warnings.
18	    def _reject(self, request, reason):
19	        return None
20	
21	
22	requires_csrf_token = decorator_from_middleware(_EnsureCsrfToken)
23	requires_csrf_token.__name__ = "requires_csrf_token"
24	requires_csrf_token.__doc__ = """
25	Use this decorator on views that need a correct csrf_token available to
26	RequestContext, but without the CSRF protection that csrf_protect
27	enforces.
28	"""
29	
30	
31	class _EnsureCsrfCookie(CsrfViewMiddleware):
32	    def _reject(self, request, reason):
33	        return None
34	
35	    def process_view(self, request, callback, callback_args, callback_kwargs):
36	        retval = super().process_view(request, callback, callback_args, callback_kwargs)
37	        # Force process_response to send the cookie
38	        get_token(request)
39	        return retval
40	
41	
42	ensure_csrf_cookie = decorator_from_middleware(_EnsureCsrfCookie)
43	ensure_csrf_cookie.__name__ = "ensure_csrf_cookie"
44	ensure_csrf_cookie.__doc__ = """
45	Use this decorator to ensure that a view sets a CSRF cookie, whether or not it
46	uses the csrf_token template tag, or the CsrfViewMiddleware is used.
47	"""
48	
49	
50	def csrf_exempt(view_func):
51	    """Mark a view function as being exempt from the CSRF view protection."""
52	
53	    # view_func.csrf_exempt = True would also work, but decorators are nicer
54	    # if they don't have side effects, so return a new function.
55	
56	    if iscoroutinefunction(view_func):
57	
58	        async def _view_wrapper(request, *args, **kwargs):
59	            return await view_func(request, *args, **kwargs)
60	
61	    else:
62	
63	        def _view_wrapper(request, *args, **kwargs):
64	            return view_func(request, *args, **kwargs)
65	
66	    _view_wrapper.csrf_exempt = True
67	
68	    return wraps(view_func)(_view_wrapper)

935	    def promote_joins(self, aliases):

938	        an outer join. If 'unconditional' is False, only promote the join if
939	        it is nullable or the parent join is an outer join.
940	
941	        The children promotion is done to avoid join chains that contain a
942	        LOUTER b INNER c. So, if we have currently a INNER b INNER c and a->b
943	        is promoted, then we must also promote b->c automatically, or otherwise
944	        the promotion of a->b doesn't actually change anything in the query
945	        results.
946	        """
947	        aliases = list(aliases)
948	        while aliases:
949	            alias = aliases.pop(0)
950	            if self.alias_map[alias].join_type is None:
951	                # This is the base table (first FROM entry) - this table
952	                # isn't really joined at all in the query, so we should not
953	                # alter its join type.
954	                continue

956	            assert self.alias_map[alias].join_type is not None
957	            parent_alias = self.alias_map[alias].parent_alias
958	            parent_louter = (
959	                parent_alias and self.alias_map[parent_alias].join_type == LOUTER
960	            )
961	            already_louter = self.alias_map[alias].join_type == LOUTER
962	            if (self.alias_map[alias].nullable or parent_louter) and not already_louter:

965	                # refer to this one.

967	                    join

980	        demote a->b automatically, or otherwise the demotion of b->c doesn't
981	        actually change anything in the query results. .

983	        aliases = list(aliases)

989	                if self.alias_map[parent_alias].join_type == INNER:

1058	    def bump_prefix(self, other_query, exclude=None):
1059	        """
1060	        Change the alias prefix to the next letter in the alphabet in a way
1061	        that the other query's aliases and this query's aliases will not
1062	        conflict. Even tables that previously had no alias will get an alias
1063	        after this call. To prevent changing aliases use the exclude parameter.
1064	        """
1065	
1066	        def prefix_gen():
1067	            """
1068	            Generate a sequence of characters in alphabetical order:
1069	                -> 'A', 'B', 'C', ...
1070	
1071	            When the alphabet is finished, the sequence will continue with the
1072	            Cartesian product:
1073	                -> 'AA', 'AB', 'AC', ...
1074	            """
1075	            alphabet = ascii_uppercase
1076	            prefix = chr(ord(self.alias_prefix) + 1)
1077	            yield prefix
1078	            for n in count(1):
1079	                seq = alphabet[alphabet.index(prefix) :] if prefix else alphabet
1080	                for s in product(seq, repeat=n):
1081	                    yield "".join(s)
1082	                prefix = None
1083	
1084	        if self.alias_prefix != other_query.alias_prefix:
1085	            # No clashes between self and outer query should be possible.
1086	            return
1087	
1088	        # Explicitly avoid infinite loop. The constant divider is based on how
1089	        # much depth recursive subquery references add to the stack. This value
1090	        # might need to be adjusted when adding or removing function calls from
1091	        # the code path in charge of performing these operations.
1092	        local_recursion_limit = sys.getrecursionlimit() // 16
1093	        for pos, prefix in enumerate(prefix_gen()):
1094	            if prefix not in self.subq_aliases:
1095	                self.alias_prefix = prefix
1096	                break
1097	            if pos > local_recursion_limit:
1098	                raise RecursionError(
1099	                    "Maximum recursion depth exceeded: too many subqueries."
1100	                )
1101	        self.subq_aliases = self.subq_aliases.union([self.alias_prefix])
1102	        other_query.subq_aliases = other_query.subq_aliases.union(self.subq_aliases)
1103	        if exclude is None:
1104	            exclude = {}
1105	        self.change_aliases(
1106	            {
1107	                alias: "%s%d" % (self.alias_prefix, pos)
1108	                for pos, alias in enumerate(self.alias_map)
1109	                if alias not in exclude
1110	            }
1111	        )

1140

1142	        reusable, or it can be a set containing the aliases that can be reused.
1143	
1144	        A join is always created as LOUTER if the lhs alias is LOUTER to make
1145	        sure chains like t1 LOUTER t2 INNER t3 aren't generated. All new
1146	        joins are created as LOUTER if the join is nullable.

1196	        of None -> some alias. This will be returned in the no-op case.
1197	        """

32	        # Apply a list/tuple of decorators if 'decorators' is one. Decorator
33	        # functions are applied so that the call order is the same as the
34	        # order in which they appear in the iterable.
35	        decorators = decorators[::-1]

61	def method_decorator(decorator, name=""):
62	    """
63	    Convert a function decorator into a method decorator
64	    """

66	    # 'obj' can be a class or a function. If 'obj' is a function at the time it
67	    # is passed to _dec,  it will eventually be a method of the class it is
68	    # defined on. If 'obj' is a class, the 'name' is required to be the name
69	    # of the method that will be decorated.
70	    def _dec(obj):
71	        if not isinstance(obj, type):
72	            return _multi_decorate(decorator, obj)
73	        if not (name and hasattr(obj, name)):
74	            raise ValueError(
75	                "The keyword argument `name` must be the name of a method "
76	                "of the decorated class: %s. Got '%s' instead." % (obj, name)
77	            )
78	        method = getattr(obj, name)
79	        if not callable(method):
80	            raise TypeError(
81	                "Cannot decorate '%s' as it isn't a callable attribute of "
82	                "%s (%s)." % (name, obj, method)
83	            )
84	        _wrapper = _multi_decorate(decorator, method)
85	        setattr(obj, name, _wrapper)
86	        return obj

88	    # Don't worry about making _dec look similar to a list/tuple as it's rather
89	    # meaningless.
90	    if not hasattr(decorator, "__iter__"):
91	        update_wrapper(_dec, decorator)
92	    # Change the name to aid debugging.
93	    obj = decorator if hasattr(decorator, "__name__") else decorator.__class__
94	    _dec.__name__ = "method_decorator(%s)" % obj.__name__
95	    return _dec

4	SimpleCookie = cookies.SimpleCookie

7	def parse_cookie(cookie):
8	    """
9	    Return a dictionary parsed from a `Cookie:` header string.
10	    """
11	    cookiedict = {}
12	    for chunk in cookie.split(";"):
13	        if "=" in chunk:
14	            key, val = chunk.split("=", 1)
15	        else:
16	            # Assume an empty name per
17	            # https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=169091
18	            key, val = "", chunk
19	        key, val = key.strip(), val.strip()
20	        if key or val:
21	            # unquote using Python's algorithm.
22	            cookiedict[key] = cookies._unquote(val)
23	    return cookiedict

124	            val = self[key]
125	        except KeyError:
126	            return default
127	        if val == []:
128	            return default
129	        return val

131	    def _getlist(self, key, default=None, force_list=False):
132	        """
133	        Return a list of values for the key.
134	
135	        Used internally to manipulate values list. If force_list is True,
136	        return a new copy of values.
137	        """
138	        try:
139	            values = super().__getitem__(key)
140	        except KeyError:
141	            if default is None:
142	                return []
143	            return default
144	        else:
145	            if force_list:
146	                values = list(values) if values is not None else None
147	            return values
148	
149	    def getlist(self, key, default=None):
150	        """
151	        Return the list of values for the key. If key doesn't exist, return a
152	        default value.
153	        """
154	        return self._getlist(key, default, force_list=True)
155	
156	    def setlist(self, key, list_):
157	        super().__setitem__(key, list_)
158	
159	    def setdefault(self, key, default=None):
160	        if key not in self:
161	            self[key] = default
162	            # Do not return default here because __setitem__() may store
163	            # another value -- QueryDict.__setitem__() does. Look it up.
164	        return self[key]
165	
166	    def setlistdefault(self, key, default_list=None):
167	        if key not in self:
168	            if default_list is None:
169	                default_list = []
170	            self.setlist(key, default_list)
171	            # Do not return default_list here because setlist() may store
172	            # another value -- QueryDict.setlist() does. Look it up.
173	        return self._getlist(key)
174	
175	    def appendlist(self, key, value):
176	        """Append an item to the internal list associated with key."""
177	        self.setlistdefault(key).append(value)
178	
179	    def items(self):
180	        """
181	        Yield (key, value) pairs, where value is the last item in the list
182	        associated with the key.
183	        """
184	        for key in self:
185	            yield key, self[key]
186	
187	    def lists(self):
188	        """Yield (key, list) pairs."""
189	        return iter(super().items())
190	
191	    def values(self):
192	        """Yield the last value on every key list."""
193	        for key in self:
194	            yield self[key]
195	
196	    def copy(self):
197	        """Return a shallow copy of this object."""
198	        return copy.copy(self)
199	
200	    def update(self, *args, **kwargs):
201	        """Extend rather than replace existing key lists."""
202	        if len(args) > 1:
203	            raise TypeError("update expected at most 1 argument, got %d" % len(args))
204	        if args:
205	            arg = args[0]
206	            if isinstance(arg, MultiValueDict):
207	                for key, value_list in arg.lists():
208	                    self.setlistdefault(key).extend(value_list)
209	            else:
210	                if isinstance(arg, Mapping):
211	                    arg = arg.items()
212	                for key, value in arg:
213	                    self.setlistdefault(key).append(value)
214	        for key, value in kwargs.items():
215	            self.setlistdefault(key).append(value)
216	
217	    def dict(self):
218	        """Return current object as a dict with singular values."""
219	        return {key: self[key] for key in self}

222	class ImmutableList(tuple):
223	    """
224	    A tuple-like object that raises useful errors when it is asked to mutate.
225	
226	    Example::
227	
228	        >>> a = ImmutableList(range(5), warning="You cannot mutate this.")
229	        >>> a[3] = '4'
230	        Traceback (most recent call last):
231	            ...
232	        AttributeError: You cannot mutate this.
233	    """
234	
235	    def __new__(cls, *args, warning="ImmutableList object is immutable.", **kwargs):
236	        self = tuple.__new__(cls, *args, **kwargs)
237	        self.warning = warning
238	        return self
239	
240	    def complain(self, *args, **kwargs):
241	        raise AttributeError(self.warning)
242	
243	    # All list mutation functions complain.
244	    __delitem__ = complain
245	    __delslice__ = complain
246	    __iadd__ = complain
247	    __imul__ = complain
248	    __setitem__ = complain
249	    __setslice__ = complain
250	    append = complain

1729	        else:

259	class IfChangedNode(Node):
260	    child_nodelists = ("nodelist_true", "nodelist_false")
261	
262	    def __init__(self, nodelist_true, nodelist_false, *varlist):
263	        self.nodelist_true = nodelist_true
264	        self.nodelist_false = nodelist_false
265	        self._varlist = varlist
266	
267	    def render(self, context):
268	        # Init state storage
269	        state_frame = self._get_context_stack_frame(context)
270	        state_frame.setdefault(self)
271	
272	        nodelist_true_output = None
273	        if self._varlist:
274	            # Consider multiple parameters. This behaves like an OR evaluation
275	            # of the multiple variables.
276	            compare_to = [
277	                var.resolve(context, ignore_failures=True) for var in self._varlist
278	            ]
279	        else:
280	            # The "{% ifchanged %}" syntax (without any variables) compares
281	            # the rendered output.
282	            compare_to = nodelist_true_output = self.nodelist_true.render(context)
283	
284	        if compare_to != state_frame[self]:
285	            state_frame[self] = compare_to
286	            # render true block if not already rendered
287	            return nodelist_true_output or self.nodelist_true.render(context)
288	        elif self.nodelist_false:
289	            return self.nodelist_false.render(context)
290	        return ""
291	
292	    def _get_context_stack_frame(self, context):
293	        # The Context object behaves like a stack where each template tag can
294	        # create a new scope. Find the place where to store the state to detect
295	        # changes.
296	        if "forloop" in context:
297	            # Ifchanged is bound to the local for loop.
298	            # When there is a loop-in-loop, the state is bound to the inner
299	            # loop, so it resets when the outer loop continues.
300	            return context["forloop"]
301	        else:
302	            # Using ifchanged outside loops. Effectively this is a no-op
303	            # because the state is associated with 'self'.
304	            return context.render_context

307	class IfNode(Node):
308	    def __init__(self, conditions_nodelists):
309	        self.conditions_nodelists = conditions_nodelists
310	
311	    def __repr__(self):
312	        return "<%s>" % self.__class__.__name__
313	
314	    def __iter__(self):
315	        for _, nodelist in self.conditions_nodelists:
316	            yield from nodelist
317	
318	    @property
319	    def nodelist(self):
320	        return NodeList(self)
321	
322	    def render(self, context):
323	        for condition, nodelist in self.conditions_nodelists:
324	            if condition is not None:  # if / elif clause
325	                try:
326	                    match = condition.eval(context)
327	                except VariableDoesNotExist:
328	                    match = None
329	            else:  # else clause
330	                match = True
331	
332	            if match:
333	                return nodelist.render(context)
334	
335	        return ""

17	class Library:
18	    """
19	    A class for registering template tags and filters. Compiled filter and
20	    template tag functions are stored in the filters and tags attributes.
21	    The filter, simple_tag, and inclusion_tag methods provide a convenient
22	    way to register callables as tags.
23	    """
24	
25	    def __init__(self):
26	        self.filters = {}
27	        self.tags = {}
28	
29	    def tag(self, name=None, compile_function=None):
30	        if name is None and compile_function is None:
31	            # @register.tag()
32	            return self.tag_function
33	        elif name is not None and compile_function is None:
34	            if callable(name):
35	                # @register.tag
36	                return self.tag_function(name)
37	            else:
38	                # @register.tag('somename') or @register.tag(name='somename')
39	                def dec(func):
40	                    return self.tag(name, func)
41	
42	                return dec
43	        elif name is not None and compile_function is not None:
44	            # register.tag('somename', somefunc)
45	            self.tags[name] = compile_function
46	            return compile_function
47	        else:
48	            raise ValueError(
49	                "Unsupported arguments to Library.tag: (%r, %r)"
50	                % (name, compile_function),
51	            )
52	
53	    def tag_function(self, func):
54	        self.tags[func.__name__] = func
55	        return func
56	
57	    def filter(self, name=None, filter_func=None, **flags):
58	        """
59	        Register a callable as a template filter. Example:
60	
61	        @register.filter
62	        def lower(value):
63	            return value.lower()
64	        """
65	        if name is None and filter_func is None:
66	            # @register.filter()
67	            def dec(func):
68	                return self.filter_function(func, **flags)
69	
70	            return dec
71	        elif name is not None and filter_func is None:
72	            if callable(name):
73	                # @register.filter
74	                return self.filter_function(name, **flags)
75	            else:
76	                # @register.filter('somename') or
77	                # @register.filter(name='somename')
78	                def dec(func):
79	                    return self.filter(name, func, **flags)
80	
81	                return dec
82	        elif name is not None and filter_func is not None:
83	            # register.filter('somename', somefunc)
84	            self.filters[name] = filter_func
85	            for attr in ("expects_localtime", "is_safe", "needs_autoescape"):
86	                if attr in flags:
87	                    value = flags[attr]
88	                    # set the flag on the filter for FilterExpression.resolve
89	                    setattr(filter_func, attr, value)
90	                    # set the flag on the innermost decorated function

92	                    setattr(unwrap(filter_func), attr, value)

95	        else:
96	            raise ValueError(
97	                "Unsupported arguments to Library.filter: (%r, %r)"
98	                % (name, filter_func),
99	            )

102	        return self.filter(func.__name__, func, **flags)

104	    def simple_tag(self, func=None, takes_context=None, name=None):
105	        """

107	
108	        @register.simple_tag

111	        """
112	
113	        def dec(func):
114	            (
115	                params,
116	                varargs,
117	                varkw,
118	                defaults,
119	                kwonly,
120	                kwonly_defaults,
121	                _,
122	            ) = getfullargspec(unwrap(func))

131	                        "must have a first argument of 'context'"
132	                    )

134	            @wraps(func)
135	            def compile_func(parser, token):
136	                bits = token.split_contents()[1:]
137	                target_var = None
138	                if len(bits) >= 2 and bits[-2] == "as":
139	                    target_var = bits[-1]

167	        """

169	
170	        @register.simple_block_tag
171	        def hello(content):

173	        """

176	            nonlocal end_name
177	            (
178	                params,
179	                varargs,
180	                varkw,
181	                defaults,
182	                kwonly,
183	                kwonly_defaults,

199	                    )

201	                if params and params[0] == "context":

223	                nodelist = parser.parse((end_name,))

313	class TagHelperNode(Node):
314	    """
315	    Base class for tag helper nodes such as SimpleNode and InclusionNode.
316	    Manages the positional and keyword arguments to be passed to the decorated
317	    function.
318	    """
319	
320	    def __init__(self, func, takes_context, args, kwargs):
321	        self.func = func
322	        self.takes_context = takes_context
323	        self.args = args
324	        self.kwargs = kwargs
325	
326	    def get_resolved_arguments(self, context):
327	        resolved_args = [var.resolve(context) for var in self.args]
328	        if self.takes_context:
329	            resolved_args = [context, *resolved_args]
330	        resolved_kwargs = {k: v.resolve(context) for k, v in self.kwargs.items()}
331	        return resolved_args, resolved_kwargs

334	class SimpleNode(TagHelperNode):
335	    child_nodelists = ()

337	    def __init__(self, func, takes_context, args, kwargs, target_var):
338	        super().__init__(func, takes_context, args, kwargs)
339	        self.target_var = target_var
340	
341	    def render(self, context):
342	        resolved_args, resolved_kwargs = self.get_resolved_arguments(context)
343	        output = self.func(*resolved_args, **resolved_kwargs)
344	        if self.target_var is not None:
345	            context[self.target_var] = output
346	            return ""
347	        if context.autoescape:
348	            output = conditional_escape(output)
349	        return output

359

364	        )

386	                t = self.filename
387	            elif isinstance(getattr(self.filename, "template", None), Template):
388	                t = self.filename.template
389	            elif not isinstance(self.filename, str) and isinstance(
390	                self.filename, Iterable
391	            ):
392	                t = context.template.engine.select_template(self.filename)
393	            else:
394	                t = context.template.engine.get_template(self.filename)
395	            context.render_context[self] = t
396	        new_context = context.new(_dict)
397	        # Copy across the CSRF token, if present, because inclusion tags are
398	        # often used for forms, and we need instructions for using CSRF
399	        # protection to be as simple as possible.
400	        csrf_token = context.get("csrf_token")
401	        if csrf_token is not None:
402	            new_context["csrf_token"] = csrf_token
403	        return t.render(new_context)

406	def parse_bits(
407	    parser,
408	    bits,
409	    params,
410	    varargs,
411	    varkw,
412	    defaults,
413	    kwonly,
414	    kwonly_defaults,
415	    name,
416	):
417	    """
418	    Parse bits for template tag helpers simple_tag and inclusion_tag, in
419	    particular by detecting syntax errors and by extracting positional and
420	    keyword arguments.
421	    """
422	    args = []
423	    kwargs = {}
424	    unhandled_params = list(params)
425	    unhandled_kwargs = [
426	        kwarg for kwarg in kwonly if not kwonly_defaults or kwarg not in kwonly_defaults
427	    ]
428	    for bit in bits:
429	        # First we try to extract a potential kwarg from the bit
430	        kwarg = token_kwargs([bit], parser)
431	        if kwarg:
432	            # The kwarg was successfully extracted
433	            param, value = kwarg.popitem()
434	            if param not in params and param not in kwonly and varkw is None:
435	                # An unexpected keyword argument was supplied
436	                raise TemplateSyntaxError(
437	                    "'%s' received unexpected keyword argument '%s'" % (name, param)
438	                )
439	            elif param in kwargs:
440	                # The keyword argument has already been supplied once
441	                raise TemplateSyntaxError(
442	                    "'%s' received multiple values for keyword argument '%s'"
443	                    % (name, param)
444	                )
445	            else:
446	                # All good, record the keyword argument
447	                kwargs[str(param)] = value
448	                if param in unhandled_params:
449	                    # If using the keyword syntax for a positional arg, then
450	                    # consume it.
451	                    unhandled_params.remove(param)
452	                elif param in unhandled_kwargs:
453	                    # Same for keyword-only arguments
454	                    unhandled_kwargs.remove(param)
455	        else:
456	            if kwargs:
457	                raise TemplateSyntaxError(
458	                    "'%s' received some positional argument(s) after some "
459	                    "keyword argument(s)" % name
460	                )
461	            else:
462	                # Record the positional argument
463	                args.append(parser.compile_filter(bit))
464	                try:
465	                    # Consume from the list of expected positional arguments
466	                    unhandled_params.pop(0)
467	                except IndexError:
468	                    if varargs is None:
469	                        raise TemplateSyntaxError(
470	                            "'%s' received too many positional arguments" % name
471	                        )
472	    if defaults is not None:
473	        # Consider the last n params handled, where n is the
474	        # number of defaults.
475	        unhandled_params = unhandled_params[: -len(defaults)]
476	    if unhandled_params or unhandled_kwargs:
477	        # Some positional arguments were not supplied
478	        raise TemplateSyntaxError(
479	            "'%s' did not receive value(s) for the argument(s): %s"
480	            % (name, ", ".join("'%s'" % p for p in unhandled_params + unhandled_kwargs))
481	        )
482	    return args, kwargs

984	    def get_default_columns(
985	        self, select_mask, start_alias=None, opts=None, from_parent=None
986	    ):
987	        """
988	        Compute the default columns for selecting every field in the base
989	        model. Will sometimes be called to pull in related models (e.g. via
990	        select_related), in which case "opts" and "start_alias" will be given
991	        to provide a starting point for the traversal.
992	
993	        Return a list of strings, quoted appropriately for use in SQL
994	        directly, as well as a set of aliases used in the select statement (if
995	        'as_pairs' is True, return a list of (alias, col_name) pairs instead
996	        of strings as the first component and None as the second component).
997	        """
998	        result = []
999	        if opts is None:
1000	            if (opts := self.query.get_meta()) is None:
1001	                return result
1002	        start_alias = start_alias or self.query.get_initial_alias()
1003	        # The 'seen_models' is used to optimize checking the needed parent
1004	        # alias for a given field. This also includes None -> start_alias to
1005	        # be used by local fields.
1006	        seen_models = {None: start_alias}
1007	        select_mask_fields = set(composite.unnest(select_mask))
1008	
1009	        for field in opts.concrete_fields:
1010	            model = field.model._meta.concrete_model
1011	            # A proxy model will have a different model and concrete_model. We
1012	            # will assign None if the field belongs to this model.
1013	            if model == opts.model:
1014	                model = None
1015	            if (
1016	                from_parent
1017	                and model is not None
1018	                and issubclass(
1019	                    from_parent._meta.concrete_model, model._meta.concrete_model
1020	                )
1021	            ):
1022	                # Avoid loading data for already loaded parents.
1023	                # We end up here in the case select_related() resolution
1024	                # proceeds from parent model to child model. In that case the
1025	                # parent model data is already present in the SELECT clause,
1026	                # and we want to avoid reloading the same data again.
1027	                continue
1028	            if select_mask and field not in select_mask_fields:
1029	                continue
1030	            alias = self.query.join_parent_model(opts, model, start_alias, seen_models)
1031	            column = field.get_col(alias)
1032	            result.append(column)
1033	        return result

1035	    def get_distinct(self):
1036	        """
1037	        Return a quoted list of fields to use in DISTINCT ON part of the query.
1038	
1039	        This method can alter the tables in the query, and thus it must be
1040	        called before get_from_clause().
1041	        """
1042	        result = []
1043	        params = []
1044	        opts = self.query.get_meta()
1045	
1046	        for name in self.query.distinct_fields:
1047	            parts = name.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
1048	            _, targets, alias, joins, path, _, transform_function = self._setup_joins(
1049	                parts, opts, None
1050	            )
1051	            targets, alias, _ = self.query.trim_joins(targets, joins, path)
1052	            for target in targets:
1053	                if name in self.query.annotation_select:
1054	                    result.append(self.connection.ops.quote_name(name))
1055	                else:
1056	                    r, p = self.compile(transform_function(target, alias))
1057	                    result.append(r)
1058	                    params.append(p)
1059	        return result, params

1061	    def find_ordering_name(
1062	        self, name, opts, alias=None, default_order="ASC", already_seen=None
1063	    ):
1064	        """
1065	        Return the table alias (the name might be ambiguous, the alias will
1066	        not be) and column name for ordering by the given 'name' parameter.
1067	        The 'name' is of the form 'field1__field2__...__fieldN'.
1068	        """
1069	        name, order = get_order_dir(name, default_order)
1070	        descending = order == "DESC"
1071	        pieces = name.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
1072	        (
1073	            field,
1074	            targets,
1075	            alias,
1076	            joins,
1077	            path,
1078	            opts,
1079	            transform_function,
1080	        ) = self._setup_joins(pieces, opts, alias)
1081	
1082	        # If we get to this point and the field is a relation to another model,
1083	        # append the default ordering for that model unless it is the pk
1084	        # shortcut or the attribute name of the field that is specified or
1085	        # there are transforms to process.
1086	        if (
1087	            field.is_relation
1088	            and opts.ordering
1089	            and getattr(field, "attname", None) != pieces[-1]
1090	            and name != "pk"
1091	            and not getattr(transform_function, "has_transforms", False)
1092	        ):
1093	            # Firstly, avoid infinite loops.
1094	            already_seen = already_seen or set()
1095	            join_tuple = tuple(
1096	                getattr(self.query.alias_map[j], "join_cols", None) for j in joins
1097	            )
1098	            if join_tuple in already_seen:
1099	                raise FieldError("Infinite loop caused by ordering.")

5	def get_template(template_name, using=None):
6	    """
7	    Load and return a template for the given name.
8	
9	    Raise TemplateDoesNotExist if no such template exists.
10	    """
11	    chain = []
12	    engines = _engine_list(using)
13	    for engine in engines:
14	        try:
15	            return engine.get_template(template_name)
16	        except TemplateDoesNotExist as e:
17	            chain.append(e)
18	
19	    raise TemplateDoesNotExist(template_name, chain=chain)
20	
21	
22	def select_template(template_name_list, using=None):
23	    """
24	    Load and return a template for one of the given names.
25	
26	    Try names in order and return the first template found.
27	
28	    Raise TemplateDoesNotExist if no such template exists.
29	    """
30	    if isinstance(template_name_list, str):
31	        raise TypeError(
32	            "select_template() takes an iterable of template names but got a "
33	            "string: %r. Use get_template() if you want to load a single "
34	            "template by name." % template_name_list
35	        )
36	
37	    chain = []
38	    engines = _engine_list(using)
39	    for template_name in template_name_list:
40	        for engine in engines:
41	            try:
42	                return engine.get_template(template_name)
43	            except TemplateDoesNotExist as e:
44	                chain.append(e)
45	
46	    if template_name_list:
47	        raise TemplateDoesNotExist(", ".join(template_name_list), chain=chain)
48	    else:
49	        raise TemplateDoesNotExist("No template names provided")
50

52	def render_to_string(template_name, context=None, request=None, using=None):
53	    """
54	    Load a template and render it with a context. Return a string.
55	
56	    template_name may be a string or a list of strings.
57	    """
58	    if isinstance(template_name, (list, tuple)):
59	        template = select_template(template_name, using=using)
60	    else:
61	        template = get_template(template_name, using=using)
62	    return template.render(context, request)
63

65	def _engine_list(using=None):
66	    return engines.all() if using is None else [engines[using]]

1514	    def get_converters(self, expressions):
1515	        i = 0
1516	        converters = {}
1517	
1518	        for expression in expressions:
1519	            if isinstance(expression, ColPairs):
1520	                cols = expression.get_source_expressions()
1521	                cols_converters = self.get_converters(cols)
1522	                for j, (convs, col) in cols_converters.items():
1523	                    converters[i + j] = (convs, col)
1524	                i += len(expression)
1525	            elif expression:
1526	                backend_converters = self.connection.ops.get_db_converters(expression)
1527	                field_converters = expression.get_db_converters(self.connection)
1528	                if backend_converters or field_converters:
1529	                    converters[i] = (backend_converters + field_converters, expression)
1530	                i += 1
1531	            else:
1532	                i += 1
1533	
1534	        return converters
1535	
1536	    def apply_converters(self, rows, converters):
1537	        connection = self.connection
1538	        converters = list(converters.items())
1539	        for row in map(list, rows):
1540	            for pos, (convs, expression) in converters:
1541	                value = row[pos]
1542	                for converter in convs:
1543	                    value = converter(value, expression, connection)
1544	                row[pos] = value
1545	            yield row
1546	
1547	    def has_composite_fields(self, expressions):
1548	        # Check for composite fields before calling the relatively costly
1549	        # composite_fields_to_tuples.
1550	        return any(isinstance(expression, ColPairs) for expression in expressions)
1551	
1552	    def composite_fields_to_tuples(self, rows, expressions):
1553	        col_pair_slices = [
1554	            slice(i, i + len(expression))
1555	            for i, expression in enumerate(expressions)
1556	            if isinstance(expression, ColPairs)
1557	        ]
1558	
1559	        for row in map(list, rows):
1560	            for pos in col_pair_slices:
1561	                row[pos] = (tuple(row[pos]),)
1562	
1563	            yield row

1565	    def results_iter(
1566	        self,
1567	        results=None,
1568	        tuple_expected=False,
1569	        chunked_fetch=False,
1570	        chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE,
1571	    ):
1572	        """Return an iterator over the results from executing this query."""
1573	        if results is None:
1574	            results = self.execute_sql(
1575	                MULTI, chunked_fetch=chunked_fetch, chunk_size=chunk_size
1576	            )
1577	        fields = [s[0] for s in self.select[0 : self.col_count]]
1578	        converters = self.get_converters(fields)
1579	        rows = chain.from_iterable(results)
1580	        if converters:
1581	            rows = self.apply_converters(rows, converters)
1582	        if self.has_composite_fields(fields):
1583	            rows = self.composite_fields_to_tuples(rows, fields)
1584	        if tuple_expected:
1585	            rows = map(tuple, rows)
1586	        return rows

1588	    def has_results(self):
1589	        """
1590	        Backends (e.g. NoSQL) can override this in order to use optimized
1591	        versions of "query has any results."
1592	        """
1593	        return bool(self.execute_sql(SINGLE))

1595	    def execute_sql(
1596	        self, result_type=MULTI, chunked_fetch=False, chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE
1597	    ):
1598	        """
1599	        Run the query against the database and return the result(s). The
1600	        return value depends on the value of result_type.
1601	
1602	        When result_type is:
1603	        - MULTI: Retrieves all rows using fetchmany(). Wraps in an iterator for
1604	           chunked reads when supported.
1605	        - SINGLE: Retrieves a single row using fetchone().
1606	        - ROW_COUNT: Retrieves the number of rows in the result.
1607	        - CURSOR: Runs the query, and returns the cursor object. It is the
1608	           caller's responsibility to close the cursor.
1609	        """
1610	        result_type = result_type or NO_RESULTS
1611	        try:
1612	            sql, params = self.as_sql()
1613	            if not sql:
1614	                raise EmptyResultSet
1615	        except EmptyResultSet:
1616	            if result_type == MULTI:
1617	                return iter([])
1618	            else:
1619	                return
1620	        if chunked_fetch:
1621	            cursor = self.connection.chunked_cursor()
1622	        else:
1623	            cursor = self.connection.cursor()
1624	        try:
1625	            cursor.execute(sql, params)
1626	        except Exception as e:
1627	            # Might fail for server-side cursors (e.g. connection closed)
1628	            try:
1629	                cursor.close()
1630	            except DatabaseError:
1631	                raise e from None
1632	            raise
1633	
1634	        if result_type == ROW_COUNT:

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive summary of the middleware chain implementation in Django.

## Django Middleware Chain Implementation - Complete Trace

### 1. **BaseHandler.load_middleware**
- **File**: `/home/bench/repos/django/django/core/handlers/base.py`
- **Line**: 27-104
- **Key aspects**:
  - Lines 27-104: The `load_middleware(self, is_async=False)` method
  - Line 38: Gets appropriate handler (`_get_response_async` or `_get_response`)
  - Line 39: Wraps handler with `convert_exception_to_response`
  - Lines 41-71: Iterates through `settings.MIDDLEWARE` in **reverse order**
  - Lines 43-49: Checks middleware sync/async capabilities
  - Lines 56-62: Calls `adapt_method_mode` to adapt handler if needed
  - Line 63: Instantiates middleware with adapted handler
  - Lines 79-95: Extracts hook methods (process_view, process_template_response, process_exception) and adapts them
  - Line 97: Wraps middleware instance with `convert_exception_to_response`
  - Line 101: Final adaptation of the complete chain
  - Line 104: Assigns to `_middleware_chain` (marks initialization complete)

### 2. **Hook Methods and Execution Order**

**In `_get_response` (sync flow)** - Lines 176-228:
- **process_request**: Called automatically by MiddlewareMixin.__call__ (lines 378-379 in deprecation.py)
- **process_view**: Called in lines 186-191 (before view execution)
- **View execution**: Lines 194-203
- **process_exception**: Lines 200-201 (if view raises exception)
- **process_template_response**: Lines 211-220 (if response has render method)
- **process_response**: Called by MiddlewareMixin.__call__ (lines 381-382 in deprecation.py)

**In `_get_response_async` (async flow)** - Lines 230-300:
- Same order but with `await` statements (lines 241-245 for process_view, etc.)

### 3. **convert_exception_to_response**
- **File**: `/home/bench/repos/django/django/core/handlers/exception.py`
- **Lines**: 25-61
- **Details**:
  - Lines 25-37: Documentation
  - Lines 38-50: Async version wrapper
  - Lines 51-61: Sync version wrapper
  - Wraps entire middleware/handler chain to catch exceptions
  - Converts exceptions to appropriate HTTP responses via `response_for_exception` (lines 64-160)

### 4. **Async vs Sync Adaptation**

**adapt_method_mode** - `/home/bench/repos/django/django/core/handlers/base.py`, lines 106-136:
- Line 123: Auto-detects if method is coroutine via `iscoroutinefunction`
- Lines 127-131: If target is async but method is sync → wraps with `sync_to_async` (line 131)
- Lines 132-135: If target is sync but method is async → wraps with `async_to_sync` (line 135)
- Line 136: Returns method unchanged if modes match

**make_middleware_decorator** - `/home/bench/repos/django/django/utils/decorators.py`, lines 123-199:
- Lines 124-126: Accepts middleware with optional args/kwargs
- Lines 128-137: `_pre_process_request` helper (process_request, process_view)
- Lines 139-144: `_process_exception` helper
- Lines 146-163: `_post_process_request` helper (process_template_response, process_response)
- Lines 165-195: Wraps view with async or sync wrapper depending on view type

### 5. **MiddlewareMixin Pattern**
- **File**: `/home/bench/repos/django/django/utils/deprecation.py`
- **Lines**: 346-402

**Class definition** (lines 346-361):
- Line 346: `class MiddlewareMixin:`
- Line 347: `sync_capable = True`
- Line 348: `async_capable = True`
- Lines 350-361: `__init__` stores get_response and detects if it's async (line 356)

**__call__ method** (lines 373-383):
- Line 373: Sync version
- Line 375-376: Routes to async if `async_mode` is True
- Lines 378-379: Calls `process_request` if present
- Line 380: Gets response from next handler
- Lines 381-382: Calls `process_response` if present

**__acall__ method** (lines 385-402):
- Line 385: Async version
- Lines 391-395: Calls `process_request` wrapped with `sync_to_async` (line 392)
- Line 396: Awaits response from handler
- Lines 398-401: Calls `process_response` wrapped with `sync_to_async` (line 398)

### 6. **Built-in Middleware Classes**

| Middleware | File | Line | Hooks Implemented |
|-----------|------|------|-------------------|
| **SecurityMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/middleware/security.py` | 8 | `process_request` (21), `process_response` (33) |
| **SessionMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/contrib/sessions/middleware.py` | 12 | `process_request` (18), `process_response` (22) |
| **CommonMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/middleware/common.py` | 13 | `process_request` (34), `process_response` (100) |
| **CsrfViewMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/middleware/csrf.py` | 165 | `process_request` (401), `process_view` (414), `process_response` (472) |
| **AuthenticationMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/contrib/auth/middleware.py` | 29 | `process_request` (30) |
| **MessageMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/contrib/messages/middleware.py` | 6 | `process_request` (11), `process_response` (14) |
| **XFrameOptionsMiddleware** | `/home/bench/repos/django/django/middleware/clickjacking.py` | 12 | `process_response` (25) |

**Detailed Hook Methods**:

- **SecurityMiddleware.process_request** (line 21): SSL redirect, returns response or None
- **SecurityMiddleware.process_response** (line 33): Adds security headers (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy)

- **SessionMiddleware.process_request** (line 18): Attaches session to request.session
- **SessionMiddleware.process_response** (line 22): Saves session and sets cookie

- **CommonMiddleware.process_request** (line 34): Checks disallowed user agents, URL rewriting (PREPEND_WWW)
- **CommonMiddleware.process_response** (line 100): Handles 404 redirects with APPEND_SLASH, adds Content-Length

- **CsrfViewMiddleware.process_request** (line 401): Initializes or validates CSRF cookie
- **CsrfViewMiddleware.process_view** (line 414): Validates CSRF token for non-safe methods
- **CsrfViewMiddleware.process_response** (line 472): Sets CSRF cookie if needed

- **AuthenticationMiddleware.process_request** (line 30): Attaches lazy request.user object

- **MessageMiddleware.process_request** (line 11): Initializes message storage
- **MessageMiddleware.process_response** (line 14): Saves messages to storage

- **XFrameOptionsMiddleware.process_response** (line 25): Sets X-Frame-Options header

412	            # created and registered. If remote_field is None, we're ordering

414	            # foreign class is - we'll add those accessors later in
415	            # contribute_to_class().

421	        # Give the class a docstring -- its definition.
422	        if cls.__doc__ is None:

424	                cls.__name__,

426	            )

429	            opts.label_lower

434	        if not opts.managers:
435	            if any(f.name == "objects" for f in opts.fields):
436	                raise ValueError(
437	                    "Model %s must specify a custom Manager, because it has a "
438	                    "field named 'objects'." % cls.__name__
439	                )
440	            manager = Manager()
441	            manager.auto_created = True
442	            cls.add_to_class("objects", manager)

444	        # Set the name of _meta.indexes. This can't be done in
445	        # Options.contribute_to_class() because fields haven't been added to
446	        # the model at that point.

64	def normalize_together(option_together):
65	    """
66	    option_together can be either a tuple of tuples, or a single
67	    tuple of two strings. Normalize it to a tuple of tuples, so that
68	    calling code can uniformly expect that.
69	    """
70	    try:
71	        if not option_together:
72	            return ()
73	        if not isinstance(option_together, (tuple, list)):
74	            raise TypeError
75	        first_element = option_together[0]
76	        if not isinstance(first_element, (tuple, list)):
77	            option_together = (option_together,)
78	        # Normalize everything to tuples
79	        return tuple(tuple(ot) for ot in option_together)
80	    except TypeError:
81	        # If the value of option_together isn't valid, return it
82	        # verbatim; this will be picked up by the check framework later.
83	        return option_together

670	    def get_field(self, field_name):
671	        """

673	        """
674	        try:

684	                    "so if this is an auto-created related field, it won't "

697	    def get_base_chain(self, model):
698	        """
699	        Return a list of parent classes leading to `model` (ordered from
700	        closest to most distant ancestor). This has to handle the case where
701	        `model` is a grandparent or even more distant relation.
702	        """
703	        if not self.parents:
704	            return []
705	        if model in self.parents:
706	            return [model]
707	        for parent in self.parents:
708	            res = parent._meta.get_base_chain(model)
709	            if res:
710	                res.insert(0, parent)
711	                return res
712	        return []

715	    def all_parents(self):
716	        """
717	        Return all the ancestors of this model as a tuple ordered by MRO.
718	        Useful for determining if something is an ancestor, regardless of
719	        lineage.
720	        """
721	        result = OrderedSet(self.parents)
722	        for parent in self.parents:
723	            for ancestor in parent._meta.all_parents:
724	                result.add(ancestor)
725	        return tuple(result)

734	    def get_ancestor_link(self, ancestor):
735	        """
736	        Return the field on the current model which points to the given
737	        "ancestor". This is possible an indirect link (a pointer to a parent
738	        model, which points, eventually, to the ancestor). Used when
739	        constructing table joins for model inheritance.
740	
741	        Return None if the model isn't an ancestor of this one.
742	        """
743	        if ancestor in self.parents:
744	            return self.parents[ancestor]
745	        for parent in self.parents:
746	            # Tries to get a link field from the immediate parent
747	            parent_link = parent._meta.get_ancestor_link(ancestor)
748	            if parent_link:
749	                # In case of a proxied model, the first link
750	                # of the chain to the ancestor is that parent
751	                # links
752	                return self.parents[parent] or parent_link

754	    def get_path_to_parent(self, parent):
755	        """
756	        Return a list of PathInfos containing the path from the current
757	        model to the parent model, or an empty list if parent is not a
758	        parent of the current model.
759	        """
760	        if self.model is parent:
761	            return []
762	        # Skip the chain of proxy to the concrete proxied model.
763	        proxied_model = self.concrete_model
764	        path = []
765	        opts = self
766	        for int_model in self.get_base_chain(parent):
767	            if int_model is proxied_model:
768	                opts = int_model._meta
769	            else:
770	                final_field = opts.parents[int_model]
771	                targets = (final_field.remote_field.get_related_field(),)
772	                opts = int_model._meta
773	                path.append(
774	                    PathInfo(
775	                        from_opts=final_field.model._meta,
776	                        to_opts=opts,
777	                        target_fields=targets,
778	                        join_field=final_field,
779	                        m2m=False,
780	                        direct=True,
781	                        filtered_relation=None,
782	                    )
783	                )
784	        return path

786	    def get_path_from_parent(self, parent):
787	        """
788	        Return a list of PathInfos containing the path from the parent
789	        model to the current model, or an empty list if parent is not a
790	        parent of the current model.
791	        """
792	        if self.model is parent:
793	            return []
794	        model = self.concrete_model
795	        # Get a reversed base chain including both the current and parent
796	        # models.
797	        chain = model._meta.get_base_chain(parent)
798	        chain.reverse()
799	        chain.append(model)
800	        # Construct a list of the PathInfos between models in chain.
801	        path = []
802	        for i, ancestor in enumerate(chain[:-1]):
803	            child = chain[i + 1]
804	            link = child._meta.get_ancestor_link(ancestor)
805	            path.extend(link.reverse_path_infos)
806	        return path

808	    def _populate_directed_relation_graph(self):
809	        """
810	        This method is used by each model to find its reverse objects. As this
811	        method is very expensive and is accessed frequently (it looks up every
812	        field in a model, in every app), it is computed on first access and
813	        then is set as a property on every model.
814	        """
815	        related_objects_graph = defaultdict(list)
816	
817	        all_models = self.apps.get_models(include_auto_created=True)
818	        for model in all_models:
819	            opts = model._meta
820	            # Abstract model's fields are copied to child models, hence we will
821	            # see the fields from the child models.
822	            if opts.abstract:
823	                continue
824	            fields_with_relations = (
825	                f
826	                for f in opts._get_fields(reverse=False, include_parents=False)
827	                if f.is_relation and f.related_model is not None
828	            )
829	            for f in fields_with_relations:
830	                if not isinstance(f.remote_field.model, str):
831	                    remote_label = f.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model._meta.label
832	                    related_objects_graph[remote_label].append(f)
833	
834	        for model in all_models:
835	            # Set the relation_tree using the internal __dict__. In this way
836	            # we avoid calling the cached property. In attribute lookup,
837	            # __dict__ takes precedence over a data descriptor (such as
838	            # @cached_property). This means that the _meta._relation_tree is
839	            # only called if related_objects is not in __dict__.
840	            related_objects = related_objects_graph[
841	                model._meta.concrete_model._meta.label
842	            ]
843	            model._meta.__dict__["_relation_tree"] = related_objects
844	        # It seems it is possible that self is not in all_models, so guard
845	        # against that with default for get().
846	        return self.__dict__.get("_relation_tree", EMPTY_RELATION_TREE)
847	
848	    @cached_property
849	    def _relation_tree(self):
850	        return self._populate_directed_relation_graph()

852	    def _expire_cache(self, forward=True, reverse=True):

856	            for cache_key in self.FORWARD_PROPERTIES:
857	                if cache_key in self.__dict__:
858	                    delattr(self, cache_key)
859	        if reverse and not self.abstract:
860	            for cache_key in self.REVERSE_PROPERTIES:
861	                if cache_key in self.__dict__:
862	                    delattr(self, cache_key)

870	
871	        - include_parents: include fields derived from inheritance
872	        - include_hidden:  include fields that have a related_name that
873	                           starts with a "+"

881	    def _get_fields(
882	        self,
883	        forward=True,
884	        reverse=True,
885	        include_parents=True,
886	        include_hidden=False,
887	        topmost_call=True,
888	    ):
889	        """
890	        Internal helper function to return fields of the model.
891	        * If forward=True, then fields defined on this model are returned.
892	        * If reverse=True, then relations pointing to this model are returned.
893	        * If include_hidden=True, then fields with is_hidden=True are returned.
894	        * The include_parents argument toggles if fields from parent models
895	          should be included. It has three values: True, False, and
896	          PROXY_PARENTS. When set to PROXY_PARENTS, the call will return all
897	          fields defined for the current model or any of its parents in the
898	          parent chain to the model's concrete model.
899	        """
900	        if include_parents not in (True, False, PROXY_PARENTS):
901	            raise TypeError(
902	                "Invalid argument for include_parents: %s" % (include_parents,)
903	            )

905	        # implementation and to provide a fast way for Django's internals to
906	        # access specific subsets of fields.
907	
908	        # Creates a cache key composed of all arguments
909	        cache_key = (forward, reverse, include_parents, include_hidden, topmost_call)
910	
911	        try:
912	            # In order to avoid list manipulation. Always return a shallow copy
913	            # of the results.
914	            return self._get_fields_cache[cache_key]
915	        except KeyError:
916	            pass

918	        fields = []
919	        # Recursively call _get_fields() on each parent, with the same
920	        # options provided in this call.
921	        if include_parents is not False:
922	            # In diamond inheritance it is possible that we see the same model
923	            # from two different routes. In that case, avoid adding fields from
924	            # the same parent again.
925	            parent_fields = set()
926	            for parent in self.parents:
927	                if (
928	                    parent._meta.concrete_model != self.concrete_model
